types.name: DocumentContent
types.description: The rich-text content body of a document, issue, project, initiative, project milestone, pull request, release note, automation prompt, AI prompt rules, or welcome message. Content is stored as a base64-encoded Yjs state and can be converted to Markdown or ProseMirror JSON. Each DocumentContent belongs to exactly one parent entity and supports real-time collaborative editing.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The document content in markdown format. This is a derived representation of the canonical Yjs content state.

fields.name: contentState
fields.description: The document content state as a base64-encoded Yjs state update. This is the canonical representation of the document content used for collaborative editing.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: pullRequest
fields.description: [Internal] The pull request that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: initiative
fields.description: The initiative that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: projectMilestone
fields.description: The project milestone that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: document
fields.description: The document that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: aiPromptRules
fields.description: The AI prompt rules that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: welcomeMessage
fields.description: The welcome message that the content is associated with. Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: restoredAt
fields.description: The time at which the document content was restored from a previous version in the content history. Null if the content has never been restored.

types.name: PullRequest
types.description: [Internal] A pull or merge request from a connected version control system (GitHub or GitLab). Pull requests are automatically linked to Linear issues via branch names or commit messages containing issue identifiers. They track the full lifecycle including status, reviewers, checks, and merge state, and are synced bidirectionally with the hosting provider.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The pull request's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs within the Linear app.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the pull request.

fields.name: number
fields.description: The pull request number as assigned by the hosting provider (e.g., #123 on GitHub). Unique within a repository.

fields.name: sourceBranch
fields.description: The source (head) branch of the pull request that contains the proposed changes.

fields.name: targetBranch
fields.description: The target (base) branch that the pull request will be merged into.

fields.name: headSha
fields.description: The Git SHA of the latest commit on the source branch. Updated as new commits are pushed. Null if not yet synced.

fields.name: baseSha
fields.description: The Git SHA of the base commit on the target branch that the pull request is compared against. Null if not yet synced.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the pull request on the hosting provider (e.g., a GitHub or GitLab URL).

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the pull request (open, closed, merged, or draft). Synced from the hosting provider.

fields.name: mergeStatus
fields.description: The current merge status of the pull request, synced from the hosting provider.

fields.name: mergeSettings
fields.description: Merge settings and allowed merge methods for this pull request's repository. Null if the settings have not been synced from the provider.

fields.name: openedAt
fields.description: The time at which the pull request was opened.

fields.name: mergeCommit
fields.description: The merge commit created when the pull request was merged. Null if the pull request has not been merged or if the merge commit data is not available.

fields.name: checks
fields.description: [Internal] The CI/CD checks and status checks associated with the pull request, synced from the hosting provider.

fields.name: commits
fields.description: [ALPHA] The commits included in the pull request, synced from the hosting provider. Includes metadata such as SHA, message, diff stats, and author information.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: [Internal] The Linear user who created the pull request. Null if the creator is an external user not mapped to a Linear account, or if the creator's account has been deleted.

types.name: PullRequestStatus
types.description: The status of a pull request.

types.name: PullRequestMergeSettings
types.description: [Internal] Merge settings and capabilities for a pull request's repository, including which merge methods are allowed and whether features like merge queues and auto-merge are enabled.

fields.name: isMergeQueueEnabled
fields.description: Whether a merge queue is enabled for pull requests in this repository. When enabled, merges go through an ordered queue rather than being merged directly.

fields.name: squashMergeAllowed
fields.description: Whether squash merge is allowed for this pull request's repository.

fields.name: autoMergeAllowed
fields.description: Whether auto-merge is allowed for the PR's repository.

fields.name: rebaseMergeAllowed
fields.description: Whether rebase merge is allowed for pull requests PR's repository.

fields.name: mergeCommitAllowed
fields.description: Whether merge commits are allowed for pull requests PR's repository.

fields.name: deleteBranchOnMerge
fields.description: Whether the branch will be deleted when the pull request is merged.

fields.name: mergeQueueMergeMethod
fields.description: The merge method used by the merge queue, if applicable. Null when merge queue is not enabled or the method is not specified.

types.name: PullRequestMergeMethod
types.description: The method used to merge a pull request.

types.name: PullRequestCommit
types.description: [ALPHA] A Git commit associated with a pull request, including metadata about the commit's content and authors.

fields.name: sha
fields.description: The Git commit SHA.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The full commit message.

fields.name: committedAt
fields.description: The timestamp when the commit was committed, as an ISO 8601 string.

fields.name: additions
fields.description: Number of additions in this commit.

fields.name: deletions
fields.description: Number of deletions in this commit.

fields.name: changedFiles
fields.description: The number of files changed in this commit. Null if the hosting provider did not include this information.

fields.name: isMergeCommit
fields.description: Whether this commit is a merge commit (has multiple parents). Merge commits are typically filtered out when counting diff stats.

fields.name: authorUserIds
fields.description: Linear user IDs for commit authors (includes co-authors).

fields.name: authorExternalUserIds
fields.description: External user IDs for commit authors (includes co-authors).

types.name: PullRequestCheck
types.description: [ALPHA] A CI/CD check or status check associated with a pull request (e.g., a GitHub Actions workflow run or a required status check).

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the check.

fields.name: workflowName
fields.description: The name of the CI workflow that triggered the check (e.g., 'CI' or 'Build and Test'). Null if the check is not part of a named workflow.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the check.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL to view the check details on the hosting provider. Null if no URL is available.

fields.name: isRequired
fields.description: Whether this check is required to pass before the pull request can be merged. Null if the required status is unknown.

fields.name: presentation
fields.description: How the check should be opened in the client.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.description: The date and time when the check started running. Null if the check has not started yet.

fields.name: completedAt
fields.description: The date and time when the check finished running. Null if the check has not completed yet.

types.name: PullRequestCheckPresentation
types.description: [ALPHA] How a pull request check should be opened in the client.

types.name: ProjectMilestone
types.description: A milestone within a project. Milestones break a project into phases or target checkpoints, each with its own target date and set of issues. Issues can be assigned to a milestone to track progress toward that checkpoint.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: documentContent

fields.name: targetDate
fields.description: The planned completion date of the milestone. Null if no target date is set.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project that this milestone belongs to.

fields.name: progressHistory
fields.description: [Internal] The progress history of the milestone, tracking issue completion over time.

fields.name: currentProgress
fields.description: [Internal] The current progress of the milestone, broken down by issue status category.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The order of the milestone in relation to other milestones within a project.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The project milestone's description in markdown format.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the project milestone.

fields.name: progress

fields.name: descriptionState
fields.description: [Internal] The project milestone's description as YJS state.

fields.name: issues

types.name: AiPromptRules
types.description: Custom rules that guide AI behavior for a specific scope. Rules can be defined at the workspace level, team level, integration level, or user level, and are applied hierarchically (workspace rules first, then parent team rules, then team rules). Rules contain structured content that instructs the AI assistant on how to handle specific types of prompts, such as coding agent guidance or triage intelligence configuration.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: updatedBy
fields.description: The user who last updated the AI prompt rules.

types.name: WelcomeMessage
types.description: A welcome message configuration for the workspace. When enabled, new users joining the workspace receive a notification with this message in their inbox. Each workspace has at most one welcome message. The message body is stored in a related DocumentContent entity.

The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the welcome message is enabled and should be sent to new users joining the workspace.

fields.name: updatedBy
fields.description: The user who last updated the welcome message configuration. Null if the user's account has been deleted.

types.name: Post
types.description: [Internal] A post or announcement in a team or user feed. Posts can be manually authored by users or AI-generated summaries of team activity. They support rich text content (ProseMirror), emoji reactions, threaded comments, and audio summaries. Posts are associated with either a team or a user, but not both.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The post content in markdown format.

fields.name: bodyData
fields.description: [Internal] The content of the post as a ProseMirror document. This is the canonical rich-text representation of the post body.

fields.name: writtenSummaryData
fields.description: [Internal] The structured data used to compose an AI-generated written summary post, including section content and source references.

fields.name: audioSummary
fields.description: The post content summarized for audio text-to-speech consumption. Null if no audio summary has been generated.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The post's title. Null or empty for posts that do not have a title.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The post's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the post. Null for system-generated posts.

fields.name: editedAt
fields.description: The time the post was last edited. Null if the post has not been edited since creation.

fields.name: reactionData
fields.description: Emoji reaction summary for this post, grouped by emoji type. Each entry contains the emoji name, count, and the IDs of users who reacted.

fields.name: ttlUrl
fields.description: A URL of the text-to-speech audio rendering of the post body. Null if no audio has been generated.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that the post is scoped to, for user-level feed posts. Null for team-scoped posts.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the post is scoped to, for team-level feed posts. Null for user-scoped posts.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the post, such as 'summary' for AI-generated feed summaries. Null for standard user-authored posts.

fields.name: evalLogId
fields.description: The evaluation log ID of the AI response that generated this post. Null for non-AI-generated posts.

fields.name: feedSummaryScheduleAtCreate
fields.description: The feed summary schedule cadence that was active when this post was created. Null for non-summary posts.

types.description: Type of Post

types.description: Cadence to generate feed summary

types.name: ExternalUser
types.description: An external user who interacts with Linear through an integrated external service (such as Slack, Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Salesforce, or Microsoft Teams) but does not have a Linear account. External users can create issues, post comments, and add reactions from their respective platforms. They are identified by service-specific user IDs and may optionally have an email address. External users are scoped to a single workspace.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The external user's full name.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The external user's display name. Unique within each workspace. Can match the display name of an actual user.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The external user's email address.

fields.name: avatarUrl
fields.description: A URL to the external user's avatar image. Null if no avatar is available from the external service.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the external user belongs to.

fields.name: lastSeen
fields.description: The last time the external user was seen interacting with Linear through their external service. Defaults to the creation time and is updated on subsequent interactions.

types.name: AgentSession
types.description: A session representing an AI coding agent's work on an issue or conversation. Agent sessions track the lifecycle of an agent's engagement, from creation through active work to completion or dismissal. Each session is associated with an agent user (the bot), optionally a human creator, an issue, and a comment thread where the agent posts updates. Sessions contain activities that record the agent's observable steps and can be linked to pull requests created during the work.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The human user responsible for the agent session. Null if the session was initiated via automation or by an agent user, with no responsible human user.

fields.name: appUser
fields.description: The agent user that is associated with this agent session.

fields.name: comment
fields.description: The comment this agent session is associated with.

fields.name: sourceComment
fields.description: The comment that this agent session was spawned from, if from a different thread.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue this agent session is associated with.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The agent session's unique URL slug.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the agent session, such as pending, active, awaiting input, complete, error, or stale.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.description: The time the agent session transitioned to active status and began work. Null if the session has not yet started.

fields.name: endedAt
fields.description: The time the agent session completed. Null if the session is still in progress or was dismissed before completion.

fields.name: dismissedAt
fields.description: The time a user dismissed this agent session. When dismissed, the agent is removed as delegate from the associated issue. Null if the session has not been dismissed.

fields.name: dismissedBy
fields.description: The user who dismissed the agent session. Automatically set when dismissedAt is updated. Null if the session has not been dismissed.

fields.name: activities
fields.description: Activities associated with this agent session.

fields.description: A dynamically updated plan describing the agent's execution strategy, including steps to be taken and their current status. Updated as the agent progresses through its work. Null if no plan has been set.

types.name: AgentActivity
types.description: An activity performed by or directed at an AI coding agent during a session. Activities represent the observable steps of an agent's work, including thoughts, actions (tool calls), responses, prompts from users, errors, and elicitation requests. Each activity belongs to an agent session and is associated with the user who initiated it.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: agentSession
fields.description: The agent session this activity belongs to.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The content of the activity, which varies by type (thought, action, response, prompt, error, or elicitation).

fields.name: sourceComment
fields.description: The source comment this activity is linked to. Null if the activity was not triggered by a comment.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who created this agent activity.

fields.name: sourceMetadata
fields.description: Metadata about the external source that created this agent activity.

fields.name: signal
fields.description: An optional modifier that provides additional instructions on how the activity should be interpreted.

fields.name: ephemeral
fields.description: Whether the activity is ephemeral, and should disappear after the next agent activity.

fields.name: contextualMetadata
fields.description: [Internal] Metadata about user-provided contextual information for this agent activity.

fields.name: queued
fields.description: [Internal] Whether this activity is queued for later processing. Queued activities are not sent to the agent until the session reaches a terminal state.

fields.name: sentAt
fields.description: [Internal] The time at which the prompt actually entered the conversation. Only set when the prompt did not enter the conversation immediately (i.e., it was queued and later dequeued). Null for prompts that were sent directly and for non-prompt activities.

fields.name: signalMetadata
fields.description: Metadata about this agent activity's signal.

types.name: AiPromptProgress
types.description: [Internal] Tracks the progress and state of an AI prompt workflow execution. Each progress record is associated with an issue or comment and contains the workflow type, current status, metadata about the execution, and optionally the conversation messages exchanged during the workflow. Progress records can form a hierarchy via parent-child relationships for multi-step workflows.

fields.description: [Internal] The metadata for the prompt workflow.

types.name: ActorBot
types.description: A bot actor representing a non-human entity that performed an action, such as an integration (GitHub, Slack, Zendesk), an AI assistant, or an automated workflow. Bot actors are displayed in activity feeds and history to indicate when changes were made by applications rather than users.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique identifier for the bot actor.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The source type of the bot, identifying the application or integration (e.g., 'github', 'slack', 'workflow', 'ai').

fields.name: subType
fields.description: A more specific classification within the bot type, providing additional context about the integration or application variant.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The display name of the bot.

fields.name: userDisplayName
fields.description: The display name of the external user on behalf of whom the bot acted. Shown when an integration action was triggered by a specific person in the external system.

fields.name: avatarUrl
fields.description: A URL pointing to the avatar image representing this bot, typically the integration's logo or icon.

types.name: SyncedExternalThread
types.description: A comment thread that is synced with an external source such as Slack, Jira, GitHub, Salesforce, or email. Provides information about the external thread's origin, its current sync status, and whether the user has the necessary personal integration connected to participate in the thread.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of this synced external thread. Auto-generated if not provided.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The category of the external source (e.g., 'integration' for service integrations, 'email' for email-based threads).

fields.name: subType
fields.description: The specific integration service for the external source (e.g., 'slack', 'jira', 'github', 'salesforce'). Null if the source type does not have a sub-type.

fields.name: name
fields.description: A human-readable display name for the external source (e.g., 'Slack', 'Jira', 'GitHub').

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: A human-readable display name for the thread, derived from the external source. For Slack threads this is the channel name, for Jira it's the issue key, for email it's the sender name and count of other participants.

fields.name: url
fields.description: A URL linking to the thread in the external service. For example, a Slack message permalink, a Jira issue URL, or a GitHub issue URL.

fields.name: isConnected
fields.description: Whether this thread is currently syncing comments bidirectionally with the external service. False if the external entity relation has been removed or if the thread was explicitly unsynced.

fields.name: isPersonalIntegrationConnected
fields.description: Whether the current user has a working personal integration connected for the external service. For example, whether they have connected their personal Jira, GitHub, or Slack account. A connected personal integration may still return false if it has an authentication error.

fields.name: isPersonalIntegrationRequired
fields.description: Whether a connected personal integration is required to post comments in this synced thread. True for Jira and GitHub threads, where comments must be attributed to a specific user in the external system. False for Slack and other services where the workspace integration can post on behalf of users.

types.name: ExternalEntityInfo

fields.name: id

fields.name: service
fields.description: The external service that this entity belongs to (e.g., jira, github, slack).

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata about the external entity. The concrete type depends on the service (e.g., Jira issue key, GitHub repo and issue number, Slack channel info). Null for entity types that do not have additional metadata.

types.name: InitiativeLabel
types.description: A label that can be applied to initiatives for categorization. Initiative labels are workspace-level and can be organized into groups with a parent-child hierarchy. Only child labels (not group labels) can be directly applied to initiatives.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The label's name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The label's description.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The label's color as a HEX string (e.g., '#EB5757'). Used for visual identification of the label in the UI.

fields.name: isGroup
fields.description: Whether the label is a group. When true, this label acts as a container for child labels and cannot be directly applied to issues or projects. When false, the label can be directly applied.

fields.name: lastAppliedAt
fields.description: The date when the label was last applied to an issue, project, or initiative. Null if the label has never been applied.

fields.name: retiredAt
fields.description: [Internal] When the label was retired.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the initiative label belongs to.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the label.

fields.name: retiredBy
fields.description: The user who retired the label. Retired labels cannot be applied to new initiatives but remain on existing ones. Null if the label is active.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent label group. If set, this label is a child within a group. Only one child label from each group can be applied to an initiative at a time.

types.name: Template
types.description: A reusable template for creating issues, projects, or documents. Templates store pre-filled field values and content as JSON data. They can be scoped to a specific team or shared across the entire workspace. Team-scoped templates may be inherited from parent teams.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The entity type this template is for, such as 'issue', 'project', or 'document'.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the template.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of what the template is used for.

fields.name: icon
fields.description: The icon of the template, either a decorative icon type or an emoji string. Null if no icon has been set.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The hex color of the template icon. Null if no custom color has been set.

fields.name: templateData
fields.description: The template data as a JSON-encoded string containing the pre-filled attributes for the entity type (e.g., issue fields, project configuration, or document content).

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The sort order of the template within the templates list.

fields.name: lastAppliedAt
fields.description: The date when the template was last applied to create or update an entity. Null if the template has never been applied.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that owns this template.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the template is associated with. If null, the template is global to the workspace.

fields.name: pipeline
fields.description: The release pipeline this template is bound to. Required when the template type is 'releaseNote' and forbidden otherwise. The pipeline owns at most one release note template, which defines the format AI follows when generating release notes.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the template. Null if the creator's account has been deleted.

fields.name: lastUpdatedBy
fields.description: The user who last updated the template. Null if the user's account has been deleted.

fields.name: inheritedFrom
fields.description: The parent team template this template was inherited from. Null for original (non-inherited) templates.

fields.name: hasFormFields
fields.description: [Internal] Whether the template has form fields

types.name: ReleasePipeline
types.description: A release pipeline that defines a release workflow with ordered stages. Pipelines can be continuous (each sync creates a completed release) or scheduled (issues accumulate in a started release that is explicitly completed). Pipelines are associated with teams and can filter commits by file path patterns.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the pipeline.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The pipeline's unique slug identifier, used in URLs and for lookup by human-readable identifier instead of UUID.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the pipeline, which determines how releases are created and managed. Continuous pipelines create a completed release per sync, while scheduled pipelines accumulate issues in a started release.

fields.name: isProduction
fields.description: Whether this pipeline targets a production environment. Defaults to true. Used to distinguish production pipelines from staging or development pipelines.

fields.name: autoGenerateReleaseNotesOnCompletion
fields.description: Whether to automatically generate a release note when a release is completed. Only applies to scheduled pipelines; ignored for continuous pipelines.

fields.name: includePathPatterns
fields.description: Glob patterns to filter commits by file path. When non-empty, only commits that modify files matching at least one pattern will be included in release syncs. An empty array means all commits are included regardless of file paths.

fields.name: approximateReleaseCount
fields.description: The approximate number of non-archived releases in this pipeline. This is a denormalized count that is updated when releases are created or archived, and may not reflect the exact count at all times.

fields.name: releaseNoteTemplate
fields.description: The document template used to define the release notes format for this pipeline. AI-generated release notes follow the structure and tone of this template. Null if no template has been configured.

fields.name: latestReleaseNote
fields.description: The release note in this pipeline whose covered range ends with the most recent release.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL to the release pipeline's releases list in the Linear app.

fields.name: teams
fields.description: Teams associated with this pipeline.

types.name: ReleaseNote
types.description: A release note. The note body is stored in related document content, and the releases it covers are tracked in releaseIds.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The release note's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs for the note.

fields.name: title
fields.description: User-supplied title for the release note.

fields.name: firstRelease
fields.description: The earliest release covered by this note.

fields.name: lastRelease
fields.description: The most recent release covered by this note.

fields.name: releaseCount
fields.description: The number of releases covered by this note.

fields.name: generationStatus
fields.description: Generation status when these release notes are being auto-generated: `pending` while the LLM call is running, `completed` once it lands. `null` means the release notes were written by a user and never went through auto-generation.

fields.name: releases

types.name: Release
types.description: A release that bundles issues together for a software deployment or version. Releases belong to a release pipeline and progress through stages (e.g., planned, started, completed, canceled). Issues are associated with releases via the IssueToRelease join entity, and the release tracks lifecycle timestamps such as when it was started, completed, or canceled.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the release.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the release in plain text or markdown. Null if no description has been set.

fields.name: version
fields.description: The version identifier for this release (e.g., 'v1.2.3' or a short commit hash). Must be unique within the pipeline. Null if no version has been assigned.

fields.name: commitSha
fields.description: The Git commit SHA associated with this release. Used for SHA-based idempotency when completing releases and for linking releases to specific points in the repository history. Null if the release was created without a commit reference.

fields.name: pipeline
fields.description: The release pipeline that this release belongs to. A release always belongs to exactly one pipeline.

fields.name: stage
fields.description: The current stage of the release within its pipeline (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Changing the stage triggers lifecycle timestamp updates and may move non-closed issues to a new release when completing a scheduled pipeline release.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the release. Null if the release was created by a non-user context such as an access key or automation.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The release's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs for the release.

fields.name: startDate
fields.description: The estimated start date of the release. This is a date-only value without a time component. Automatically set to today when the release moves to a started stage if not already set. Null if no start date has been specified.

fields.name: targetDate
fields.description: The estimated completion date of the release. This is a date-only value without a time component. Null if no target date has been specified.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.description: The time at which the release first entered a started stage. Null if the release has not yet been started.

fields.name: completedAt
fields.description: The time at which the release was completed. Set automatically when the release moves to a completed stage. Reset to null if the release moves back to a non-completed stage.

fields.name: canceledAt
fields.description: The time at which the release was canceled. Set automatically when the release moves to a canceled stage. Reset to null if the release moves back to a non-canceled stage.

fields.name: trashed
fields.description: A flag that indicates whether the release is in the trash bin. Trashed releases are archived and will be permanently deleted after a retention period. Null when the release is not trashed.

fields.name: progressHistory
fields.description: The historical progress snapshots for the release, tracking how issue completion has evolved over time.

fields.name: currentProgress
fields.description: The current progress summary for the release, including counts of issues by workflow state type (e.g., completed, in progress, unstarted).

fields.name: history
fields.description: History entries associated with the release.

types.name: ReleaseStage
types.description: A stage within a release pipeline that represents a phase in the release lifecycle (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Releases progress through stages as they move toward production. Started-type stages can be frozen to prevent new issues from being automatically synced into releases at that stage.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the stage.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The display color of the stage as a HEX string (e.g., '#0f783c'), used for visual representation in the UI.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The lifecycle type of the stage (planned, started, completed, or canceled). The type determines what lifecycle timestamps are set on a release when it enters this stage.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The position of the stage within its pipeline, used for ordering stages in the UI. Lower values appear first.

fields.name: frozen
fields.description: Whether this stage is frozen. Only applicable to started-type stages. When a stage is frozen, automated release syncs will not target releases in this stage, and new issues will not be automatically added. At least one started stage in the pipeline must remain non-frozen.

fields.name: pipeline
fields.description: The release pipeline that this stage belongs to. A stage always belongs to exactly one pipeline.

fields.name: releases
fields.description: Releases associated with this stage.

types.name: WorkflowState
types.description: A state in a team's workflow, representing an issue status such as Triage, Backlog, Todo, In Progress, In Review, Done, or Canceled. Each team has its own set of workflow states that define the progression of issues through the team's process. Workflow states have a type that categorizes them (triage, backlog, unstarted, started, completed, canceled), a position that determines their display order, and a color for visual identification. States can be inherited from parent teams to sub-teams.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The state's human-readable name (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Done', 'Backlog').

fields.name: color
fields.description: The state's UI color as a HEX string.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the state.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The position of the state in the team's workflow. States are displayed in ascending order of position within their type group.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the state. One of "triage", "backlog", "unstarted", "started", "completed", "canceled", "duplicate".

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that this workflow state belongs to. Each team has its own set of workflow states.

fields.name: inheritedFrom
fields.description: The parent team's workflow state that this state was inherited from. Null if the state is not inherited from a parent team.

fields.name: issues
fields.description: Issues that currently have this workflow state. Returns a paginated and filterable list of issues.

types.name: Favorite
types.description: A user's bookmarked item that appears in their sidebar for quick access. Favorites can reference various entity types including issues, projects, cycles, views, documents, initiatives, labels, users, customers, dashboards, and pull requests. Favorites can be organized into folders and ordered by the user. Each favorite is owned by a single user and links to exactly one target entity (or is a folder containing other favorites).

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of entity this favorite references, such as 'issue', 'project', 'cycle', 'customView', 'document', 'folder', etc. Determines which associated entity field is populated.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent folder of the favorite. Null if the favorite is at the top level of the sidebar. Only favorites of type 'folder' can be parents.

fields.name: folderName
fields.description: The name of the folder. Only applies to favorites of type folder.

fields.name: projectTab
fields.description: The targeted tab of the project.

fields.name: predefinedViewType
fields.description: The type of favorited predefined view (e.g., 'allIssues', 'activeCycle', 'backlog', 'triage'). Only populated when the favorite type is 'predefinedView'.

fields.name: initiativeTab
fields.description: The targeted tab of the initiative.

fields.name: pipelineTab
fields.description: The targeted tab of the release pipeline.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: The user who owns this favorite. Favorites are personal and only visible to their owner.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The position of this item in the user's favorites list. Lower values appear first. Used to maintain user-defined ordering within the sidebar.

fields.name: children
fields.description: Children of the favorite. Only applies to favorites of type folder.

args.name: before
args.description: A cursor to be used with last for backward pagination.

args.name: after
args.description: A cursor to be used with first for forward pagination

args.name: first
args.description: The number of items to forward paginate (used with after). Defaults to 50.

args.name: last
args.description: The number of items to backward paginate (used with before). Defaults to 50.

args.name: includeArchived
args.description: Should archived resources be included (default: false)

args.name: orderBy
args.description: By which field should the pagination order by. Available options are createdAt (default) and updatedAt.

types.name: CustomView
types.description: A custom view built from a saved filter, sort, and grouping configuration. Views can be personal (visible only to the owner) or shared with the entire workspace. They define which issues, projects, initiatives, or feed items are displayed and how they are organized. Views can optionally be scoped to a team, project, or initiative.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom view, displayed in the sidebar and navigation.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the custom view.

fields.name: icon
fields.description: The icon of the custom view. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon identifier.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The hex color code of the custom view icon.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace of the custom view.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who originally created the custom view.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: The user who owns the custom view. For personal views, only the owner can see and edit the view.

fields.name: updatedBy
fields.description: The user who last updated the custom view. Null if the updater's account has been deleted.

fields.name: filters
fields.description: The legacy serialized filters applied to issues in the custom view.
fields.deprecationReason: Will be replaced by `filterData` in a future update

fields.name: filterData
fields.description: The structured filter applied to issues in the custom view. Used when the view's modelName is "Issue".

fields.name: projectFilterData
fields.description: The filter applied to projects in the custom view. When set, this view displays projects instead of issues.

fields.name: initiativeFilterData
fields.description: The filter applied to initiatives in the custom view. When set, this view displays initiatives instead of issues.

fields.name: feedItemFilterData
fields.description: The filter applied to feed items in the custom view. When set, this view displays feed items (updates) instead of issues.

fields.name: shared
fields.description: Whether the custom view is shared with everyone in the organization. Shared views appear in the workspace sidebar for all members. Personal (non-shared) views are only visible to their owner.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The custom view's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs. Automatically generated on creation.

fields.name: modelName
fields.description: The entity type this view displays. Determined by which filter is set: "Project" if projectFilterData is set, "Initiative" if initiativeFilterData is set, "FeedItem" if feedItemFilterData is set, or "Issue" by default.

fields.name: facet
fields.description: [INTERNAL] The facet that links this custom view to its parent entity (project, initiative, team page, etc.). Null if the view is not attached to any parent via a facet.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the custom view is scoped to. Null if the view is workspace-wide or scoped to a project/initiative instead.

fields.name: projects
fields.description: Projects matching the custom view's project filter. Returns an empty connection if the view's modelName is not "Project".

args.name: includeSubTeams
args.description: Include projects from sub-teams when the custom view is associated with a team.

args.name: filter
args.description: Filter returned projects.

types.name: FeedItem
types.description: [Internal] An item in a user's activity feed. Feed items represent project updates, initiative updates, or posts that appear in a user's personalized feed based on their team memberships and feed subscriptions. Each feed item references exactly one content entity. Feed items older than 6 months are automatically archived.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The organization this feed item belongs to.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The specific user this feed item is targeted to. Null if the feed item is targeted to a team or organization rather than an individual user.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team this feed item is associated with. Null if the feed item is not team-scoped.

fields.name: projectUpdate
fields.description: The project update referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.

fields.name: initiativeUpdate
fields.description: The initiative update referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.

fields.name: post
fields.description: The post referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.

inputFields.name: updatedAt

inputFields.description: Comparator for the update type: initiative, project, team

inputFields.description: Comparator for the project or initiative update health: onTrack, atRisk, offTrack

inputFields.name: projectUpdate
inputFields.description: Filters that the feed item's project update must satisfy.

inputFields.name: relatedInitiatives
inputFields.description: Filters that the related feed item initiatives must satisfy.

inputFields.name: relatedTeams
inputFields.description: Filters that the related feed item team must satisfy.

inputFields.name: and
inputFields.description: Compound filters, all of which need to be matched by the feed item.

inputFields.name: or
inputFields.description: Compound filters, one of which need to be matched by the feed item.

types.name: ViewPreferences
types.description: The display preferences for a view, controlling layout mode (list, board, spreadsheet), grouping, sorting, column visibility, and other visual settings. View preferences exist at two levels: organization-wide defaults and per-user overrides. The effective preferences are computed by merging both layers, with user preferences taking priority.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of view preferences: "organization" for workspace-wide defaults or "user" for personal overrides.

fields.name: viewType
fields.description: The type of view these preferences apply to, such as board, cycle, project, customView, myIssues, etc.

fields.name: preferences
fields.description: The view preferences values, containing layout, grouping, sorting, and field visibility settings.

types.name: ViewPreferencesValues
types.description: The computed view preferences values for a view, containing all display settings such as layout mode, grouping, sorting, field visibility, and other visual configuration. These values represent the merged result of organization defaults and user overrides.

types.name: IssueLabel
types.description: Labels that can be associated with issues. Labels help categorize and filter issues across a workspace. They can be workspace-level (shared across all teams) or team-scoped. Labels have a color for visual identification and can be organized hierarchically into groups, where a parent label acts as a group containing child labels. Labels may also be inherited from parent teams to sub-teams.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The label's name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The label's description.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The label's color as a HEX string (e.g., '#EB5757'). Used for visual identification of the label in the UI.

fields.name: isGroup
fields.description: Whether the label is a group. When true, this label acts as a container for child labels and cannot be directly applied to issues or projects. When false, the label can be directly applied.

fields.name: lastAppliedAt
fields.description: The date when the label was last applied to an issue, project, or initiative. Null if the label has never been applied.

fields.name: retiredAt
fields.description: [Internal] When the label was retired.

fields.name: organization
fields.deprecationReason: Workspace labels are identified by their team being null.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the label is scoped to. If null, the label is a workspace-level label available to all teams in the workspace.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the label.

fields.name: retiredBy
fields.description: The user who retired the label.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent label.

fields.name: inheritedFrom
fields.description: The original workspace or parent-team label that this label was inherited from. Null if the label is not inherited.

fields.name: issues
fields.description: Issues associated with the label.

args.name: filter
args.description: Filter returned issues.

args.name: before
args.description: A cursor to be used with last for backward pagination.

args.name: after
args.description: A cursor to be used with first for forward pagination

args.name: first
args.description: The number of items to forward paginate (used with after). Defaults to 50.

args.name: last
args.description: The number of items to backward paginate (used with before). Defaults to 50.

args.name: includeArchived
args.description: Should archived resources be included (default: false)

args.name: orderBy
args.description: By which field should the pagination order by. Available options are createdAt (default) and updatedAt.

fields.name: children
fields.description: Child labels within this label group. Only populated when the label is a group (isGroup is true).

args.name: filter
args.description: Filter returned issue labels.

args.name: before
args.description: A cursor to be used with last for backward pagination.

args.name: after
args.description: A cursor to be used with first for forward pagination

args.name: first
args.description: The number of items to forward paginate (used with after). Defaults to 50.

args.name: last
args.description: The number of items to backward paginate (used with before). Defaults to 50.

args.name: includeArchived
args.description: Should archived resources be included (default: false)

args.name: orderBy
args.description: By which field should the pagination order by. Available options are createdAt (default) and updatedAt.

fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: ProjectLabel
types.description: A label that can be applied to projects for categorization. Project labels are workspace-level and can be organized into groups with a parent-child hierarchy. Only child labels (not group labels) can be directly applied to projects.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The label's name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The label's description.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The label's color as a HEX string (e.g., '#EB5757'). Used for visual identification of the label in the UI.

fields.name: isGroup
fields.description: Whether the label is a group. When true, this label acts as a container for child labels and cannot be directly applied to issues or projects. When false, the label can be directly applied.

fields.name: lastAppliedAt
fields.description: The date when the label was last applied to an issue, project, or initiative. Null if the label has never been applied.

fields.name: retiredAt
fields.description: [Internal] When the label was retired.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the project label belongs to.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the label.

fields.name: retiredBy
fields.description: The user who retired the label. Retired labels cannot be applied to new projects but remain on existing ones. Null if the label is active.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent label group. If set, this label is a child within a group. Only one child label from each group can be applied to a project at a time.

fields.name: projects
fields.description: Projects associated with the label.
