types.name: Customer
types.description: A customer organization tracked in Linear's customer management system. Customers represent external companies or organizations whose product requests and feedback are captured as customer needs, which can be linked to issues and projects. Customers can be associated with domains, external system IDs, Slack channels, and managed by integrations such as Intercom or Salesforce.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The display name of the customer organization.

fields.name: logoUrl
fields.description: URL of the customer's logo image. Null if no logo has been uploaded.

fields.name: domains
fields.description: The email domains associated with this customer (e.g., 'acme.com'). Used to automatically match incoming requests to this customer. Public email domains (e.g., gmail.com) are not allowed. Domains must be unique across all customers in the workspace.

fields.name: externalIds
fields.description: Identifiers for this customer in external systems (e.g., CRM IDs from Intercom, Salesforce, or HubSpot). Used for matching customers during integration syncs and upsert operations. External IDs must be unique across customers in the workspace.

fields.name: slackChannelId
fields.description: The ID of the Slack channel linked to this customer for communication. Null if no Slack channel has been associated. Must be unique across all customers in the workspace.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: The workspace member assigned as the owner of this customer. Null if no owner has been assigned. App users cannot be set as customer owners.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current lifecycle status of the customer. Defaults to the first status by position when a customer is created without an explicit status.

fields.name: revenue
fields.description: The annual revenue generated by this customer. Null if revenue data has not been provided. May be synced from an external data source such as a CRM integration.

fields.name: size
fields.description: The number of employees or seats at the customer organization. Null if size data has not been provided. May be synced from an external data source such as a CRM integration.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The tier or segment assigned to this customer for prioritization (e.g., Enterprise, Pro, Free). Null if no tier has been assigned.

fields.name: approximateNeedCount
fields.description: The approximate count of customer needs (requests) associated with this customer. This is a denormalized counter and may not reflect the exact count at all times.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: A unique, human-readable URL slug for the customer. Automatically generated and used in customer page URLs.

fields.name: mainSourceId
fields.description: The primary external source ID when a customer has data from multiple external systems. Must be one of the values in the externalIds array. Null if the customer has zero or one external source.

fields.name: needs
fields.description: The list of customer needs (product requests and feedback) associated with this customer.

fields.name: integration
fields.description: The integration that manages this customer's data (e.g., Intercom, Salesforce). Null if the customer is not managed by any data source integration.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the customer's page in the Linear application.

types.name: CustomerStatus
types.description: A workspace-defined lifecycle status for customers (e.g., Active, Churned, Trial). Customer statuses are ordered by position and displayed with a color in the UI. Every workspace has at least one status, and a default status is assigned to new customers when none is specified.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The internal name of the status. Used as the default display name if no displayName is explicitly set.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The color of the status indicator in the UI, as a HEX string (e.g., '#ff0000').

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description explaining what this status represents in the customer lifecycle.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The sort position of the status in the workspace's customer lifecycle flow. Lower values appear first. Collisions are automatically resolved by redistributing positions.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The user-facing display name of the status shown in the UI. Defaults to the internal name if not explicitly set.

fields.name: type
fields.description: [Deprecated] The type of the customer status. Always returns null as statuses are no longer grouped by type.
fields.deprecationReason: Customer statuses are no longer grouped by type.

types.name: CustomerStatusType
types.description: [DEPRECATED] A type of customer status.

types.name: CustomerTier
types.description: A workspace-defined tier or segment for categorizing customers (e.g., Enterprise, Pro, Free). Customer tiers are used for prioritization and filtering, are ordered by position, and displayed with a color in the UI. Tier names are unique within a workspace.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The internal name of the tier. Must be unique within the workspace. Used as the default display name if no displayName is explicitly set.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The color of the tier indicator in the UI, as a HEX string (e.g., '#ff0000').

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description explaining what this tier represents and its intended use for customer segmentation.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The sort position of the tier in the workspace's customer tier ordering. Lower values appear first. Collisions are automatically resolved by redistributing positions.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The user-facing display name of the tier shown in the UI. Defaults to the internal name if not explicitly set.

types.name: CustomerNeed
types.description: A customer need represents a specific product request or piece of feedback from a customer. Customer needs serve as the bridge between customer feedback and engineering work by linking a customer to an issue or project, optionally with a comment or attachment providing additional context. Needs can be created manually, from integrations, or from intake sources like email.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: customer
fields.description: The customer organization this need belongs to. Null if the need has not yet been associated with a customer.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue this need is linked to. Either issueId or projectId must be set. When set, the need's projectId is denormalized from the issue's project.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project this need is linked to. For issue-based needs, this is denormalized from the issue's project. For project-only needs, this is set directly.

fields.name: comment
fields.description: An optional comment providing additional context for this need. Null if the need was not created from or associated with a specific comment.

fields.name: attachment
fields.description: The issue attachment linked to this need. Populated when the need originates from an intake source (e.g., Slack, Intercom) or when a URL is manually provided. Provides a link back to the original source of the customer feedback. Mutually exclusive with projectAttachment.

fields.name: projectAttachment
fields.description: The project attachment linked to this need. Populated when the need originates from an intake source or when a URL is manually provided for a project-level need. Provides a link back to the original source of the customer feedback. Mutually exclusive with attachment.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Whether the customer need is important or not. 0 = Not important, 1 = Important.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The body content of the need in Markdown format. Used to capture manual input about needs that cannot be directly tied to an attachment. Null if the need's content comes from an attached source.

fields.name: bodyData
fields.description: [Internal] The body content of the need as a Prosemirror document JSON string. This is the structured representation of the body field, used for rich text rendering in the editor.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who manually created this customer need. Null for needs created automatically by integrations or intake sources.

fields.name: originalIssue
fields.description: The issue this customer need was originally created on, before being moved to a different issue or project. Null if the customer need has not been moved from its original location.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the source attachment linked to this need, if any. Returns the URL from either the issue attachment or project attachment. Null if the need has no attached source.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The effective Markdown content shown for this customer need. Returns the manually stored body when present, otherwise falls back to content extracted from the source attachment. Null if no content is available.

types.name: Attachment
types.description: An attachment linking external content to an issue. Attachments represent connections to external resources such as GitHub pull requests, Slack messages, Zendesk tickets, Figma files, Sentry issues, Intercom conversations, and plain URLs. Each attachment has a title and subtitle displayed in the Linear UI, a URL serving as both the link destination and unique identifier per issue, and optional metadata specific to the source integration.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Content for the title line in the Linear attachment widget.

fields.name: subtitle
fields.description: Content for the subtitle line in the Linear attachment widget.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the external resource this attachment links to. Also serves as a unique identifier for the attachment within an issue; no two attachments on the same issue can share the same URL.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The creator of the attachment.

fields.name: externalUserCreator
fields.description: The non-Linear user who created the attachment.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Integration-specific metadata for this attachment. The schema varies by source type and may include fields such as pull request status, review counts, commit information, ticket status, or other data from the external system.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Information about the source which created the attachment.

fields.name: sourceType
fields.description: The source type of the attachment, derived from the source metadata. Returns the integration type (e.g., 'github', 'slack', 'zendesk') or 'unknown' if no source is set.

fields.name: groupBySource
fields.description: Whether attachments from the same source application should be visually grouped together in the Linear issue detail view.

fields.name: originalIssue
fields.description: The issue this attachment was originally created on. Null if the attachment hasn't been moved.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue this attachment belongs to.

fields.name: bodyData
fields.description: The body data of the attachment, if any.

types.name: ProjectAttachment
types.description: An attachment (link, reference, or integration data) associated with a project. Attachments are typically created by integrations and contain metadata for rendering in the client.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the attachment.

fields.name: subtitle
fields.description: Optional subtitle of the attachment, providing additional context below the title.

fields.name: url
fields.description: URL of the attachment.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The creator of the attachment.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Custom metadata related to the attachment. Contains user-facing content such as conversation messages or rendered attributes from integrations.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Metadata about the external source which created the attachment, including foreign identifiers used for syncing with external services. Null if the attachment was not created by an integration.

fields.name: sourceType
fields.description: The source type of the attachment, derived from the source metadata. Returns the integration type that created the attachment (e.g., 'slack', 'github'), or null if not set.

types.name: Integration
types.description: An integration with an external service. Integrations connect Linear to tools like Slack, GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Figma, Sentry, Zendesk, Intercom, Front, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, Google Sheets, Microsoft Teams, Discord, Salesforce, and others. Each integration record represents a single configured connection, scoped to a workspace and optionally to a specific team, project, initiative, or custom view. Personal integrations (e.g., Slack Personal, Jira Personal, GitHub Personal) are scoped to the user who created them.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: service
fields.description: The integration's type, identifying which external service this integration connects to (e.g., 'slack', 'github', 'jira', 'figma'). This determines the shape of the integration's settings and data.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the integration is associated with.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the integration is associated with.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user that added the integration.

types.name: Dashboard
types.description: [Internal] A configurable dashboard composed of widgets that display analytics, metrics, and insights. Dashboards can be personal or shared with the workspace, and optionally scoped to one or more teams. Each dashboard contains a set of widget configurations that define what data is visualized.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The dashboard's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs. Automatically generated on creation.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the dashboard.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the dashboard.

fields.name: icon
fields.description: The icon of the dashboard. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon identifier.

fields.name: color
fields.description: The hex color code of the dashboard icon.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The sort order of the dashboard within the workspace or its team. Lower values appear first.

fields.name: shared
fields.description: Whether the dashboard is shared with everyone in the workspace. Shared dashboards are visible to all members; unshared dashboards are only visible to the owner.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The organization that the dashboard belongs to.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the dashboard. Null if the creator's account has been deleted.

fields.name: updatedBy
fields.description: The user who last updated the dashboard. Null if no user has updated it or the updater's account has been deleted.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: The owner of the dashboard. For personal dashboards, only the owner can view them. Null if the owner's account has been deleted.

fields.name: issueFilter
fields.description: A global issue filter applied to all dashboard widgets that display issue data. Individual widgets may apply additional filters on top of this.

fields.name: projectFilter
fields.description: A global project filter applied to all dashboard widgets that display project data. Individual widgets may apply additional filters on top of this.

fields.name: widgets
fields.description: The widget configuration for the dashboard, defining the layout grid of rows and the widgets within each row, including their type, size, and data settings.

types.name: IssueSharedAccess
types.description: Metadata about an issue's shared access state, including which users the issue is shared with and any field restrictions for shared-only viewers.

fields.name: isShared
fields.description: Whether this issue has been shared with users outside the team.

fields.name: viewerHasOnlySharedAccess
fields.description: Whether the viewer can access this issue only through issue sharing.

fields.name: sharedWithCount
fields.description: The number of users this issue is shared with.

fields.name: sharedWithUsers
fields.description: Users this issue is shared with.

fields.name: disallowedIssueFields
fields.description: Issue update fields the viewer cannot modify due to shared-only access.

types.name: IssueSharedAccessDisallowedField
types.description: Issue update fields that are disallowed for users with only shared access.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: IssueHistory
types.description: A record of changes to an issue. Each history entry captures one or more property changes made to an issue within a short grouping window by the same actor. History entries track changes to fields such as title, assignee, status, priority, project, cycle, labels, due date, estimate, parent issue, and more. They also record metadata about what triggered the change (e.g., a user action, workflow automation, triage rule, or integration).

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue that was changed.

fields.name: actorId
fields.description: Identifier of the user who made these changes. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the change was made by an integration, automation, or system process.

fields.name: updatedDescription
fields.description: Whether the issue's description was updated.

fields.name: fromTitle
fields.description: What the title was changed from.

fields.name: toTitle
fields.description: What the title was changed to.

fields.name: fromAssigneeId
fields.description: Identifier of the user from whom the issue was re-assigned. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the assignee was not changed or the issue was previously unassigned.

fields.name: toAssigneeId
fields.description: Identifier of the user to whom the issue was assigned. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the assignee was not changed or the issue was unassigned.

fields.name: fromPriority
fields.description: What the priority was changed from.

fields.name: toPriority
fields.description: What the priority was changed to.

fields.name: fromTeamId
fields.description: Identifier of the team from which the issue was moved. Can be used to query the team directly. Null if the team was not changed.

fields.name: toTeamId
fields.description: Identifier of the team to which the issue was moved. Can be used to query the team directly. Null if the team was not changed.

fields.name: fromParentId
fields.description: Identifier of the previous parent issue. Can be used to query the issue directly. Null if the parent was not changed or the issue previously had no parent.

fields.name: toParentId
fields.description: Identifier of the new parent issue. Can be used to query the issue directly. Null if the parent was not changed or the issue was removed from its parent.

fields.name: fromStateId
fields.description: Identifier of the previous workflow state (issue status) of the issue. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Null if the status was not changed.

fields.name: toStateId
fields.description: Identifier of the new workflow state (issue status) of the issue. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Null if the status was not changed.

fields.name: fromCycleId
fields.description: Identifier of the previous cycle of the issue. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if the cycle was not changed or the issue was not in a cycle.

fields.name: toCycleId
fields.description: Identifier of the new cycle of the issue. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if the cycle was not changed or the issue was removed from a cycle.

fields.name: toConvertedProjectId
fields.description: Identifier of the new project that was created by converting this issue to a project. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the issue was not converted to a project.

fields.name: fromProjectId
fields.description: Identifier of the previous project of the issue. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the project was not changed or the issue was not in a project.

fields.name: toProjectId
fields.description: Identifier of the new project of the issue. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the project was not changed or the issue was removed from a project.

fields.name: fromEstimate
fields.description: What the estimate was changed from.

fields.name: toEstimate
fields.description: What the estimate was changed to.

fields.name: archived
fields.description: Whether the issue was archived (true) or unarchived (false) in this change. Null if the archive status was not changed.

fields.name: trashed
fields.description: Whether the issue was trashed or un-trashed.

fields.name: attachmentId
fields.description: Identifier of the attachment that was linked to or unlinked from the issue. Can be used to query the attachment directly. Null if no attachment change occurred.

fields.name: autoClosed

fields.name: autoArchived
fields.description: Whether the issue was auto-archived.

fields.name: fromDueDate

fields.name: toDueDate
fields.description: What the due date was changed to.

fields.name: actor

fields.name: actors

fields.name: descriptionUpdatedBy

fields.name: fromAssignee
fields.description: The user that was unassigned from the issue.

fields.name: toAssignee

fields.name: fromCycle
fields.description: The cycle that the issue was moved from.

fields.name: toCycle
fields.description: The cycle that the issue was moved to.

fields.name: toConvertedProject

fields.name: toDelegate

fields.name: fromProject

fields.name: toProject

fields.name: fromState

fields.name: toState
fields.description: The state that the issue was moved to.

fields.name: fromTeam

fields.name: fromParent

fields.name: id

types.name: WorkflowDefinition
types.description: An automation workflow definition that executes a set of activities when triggered by specific events. Workflows can be scoped to a team, project, cycle, label, custom view, initiative, or user context. They are triggered by entity changes (e.g., issue status change, new comment) and can include conditions that filter which events actually execute the workflow. Activities define the actions taken, such as updating issue properties, sending notifications, or posting to Slack.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the workflow.

fields.name: groupName
fields.description: The name of the group that the workflow belongs to.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the workflow.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the workflow, such as custom automation, SLA, or auto-close.

fields.name: trigger
fields.description: The event that triggers the workflow, such as entity creation, update, or a specific state change.

fields.name: triggerType
fields.description: The entity type that triggers this workflow, such as Issue, Project, or Release.

fields.name: conditions
fields.description: The filter conditions that must match for the workflow to execute. When null, the workflow triggers on all matching events.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the workflow is enabled and will execute when its trigger conditions are met.

fields.name: runOnce
fields.description: Whether the workflow should only execute once per entity. When true, the workflow is excluded from matching automations for an entity if it has already been executed for that entity.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team associated with the workflow. If not set, the workflow is associated with the entire workspace.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the workflow.

fields.name: activities
fields.description: The ordered list of activities (actions) that are executed when the workflow triggers, such as updating issue properties, sending notifications, or calling webhooks.

fields.name: sortOrder

fields.name: lastExecutedAt
fields.description: The date and time when the workflow was last triggered and executed. Null if the workflow has never been executed.

fields.name: lastUpdatedBy

fields.name: slugId

Each conversation tracks the full thread state, context references (issues, projects, documents, etc.), and conversation parts (prompts, text responses, tool calls, widgets). Conversations can originate from direct chat, issue comments, Slack threads, Microsoft Teams, or automated workflows.

The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who the conversation belongs to.

fields.name: context
fields.description: The entity contexts this conversation is related to, such as issues, projects, or documents that provide context for the AI assistant.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: A summary of the conversation.

fields.name: slugId
fields.description: The conversation's unique URL slug.

fields.name: workflowDefinition
fields.description: [Internal] The workflow definition that created this conversation.

fields.name: workflowCronJobDefinition
fields.description: [Internal] The cron workflow definition that created this conversation.

fields.name: initialSource
fields.description: The source from which this conversation was initiated, such as direct chat, an issue comment, a Slack thread, Microsoft Teams, or a workflow automation.

fields.name: clientPlatform
fields.description: The client platform from which this conversation was initiated, such as web, desktop, or mobile.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current processing status of the conversation, indicating whether the AI is actively generating a response or has completed its turn.

fields.name: readAt
fields.description: The time when the user marked the conversation as read. Null if the user hasn't read the conversation.

fields.name: parts
fields.description: The ordered sequence of conversation parts (prompts, text responses, reasoning steps, tool calls, errors, and widgets) that make up this conversation's visible history.

fields.name: iterationId
fields.description: The iteration ID when this conversation is part of an agentic workflow. Used to track multi-step workflow executions. Null for non-workflow conversations.

fields.name: evalLogId
fields.description: [Internal] The log ID of the AI response.

types.name: WorkflowCronJobDefinition
types.description: A time-triggered automation workflow definition that executes on a recurring schedule. Cron job definitions are scoped to a team and contain a set of activities (actions) that run automatically according to the configured cron schedule, such as periodically assigning issues or sending notifications.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the workflow cron job.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the workflow cron job.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the workflow cron job is enabled and will execute on its schedule.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team associated with the workflow cron job.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the workflow cron job.

fields.name: schedule
fields.description: Recurring schedule which is used to execute the workflow cron job.

fields.name: activities
fields.description: An array of activities that will be executed as part of the workflow cron job.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The sort order of the workflow cron job definition within its siblings.

types.name: IssueRelation
types.description: A relation between two issues. Issue relations represent directional relationships such as blocking, being blocked by, relating to, or duplicating another issue. Each relation connects a source issue to a related issue with a specific type describing the nature of the relationship.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of relationship between the source issue and the related issue. Possible values include blocks, duplicate, and related.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The source issue whose relationship is being described. This is the issue from which the relation originates.

fields.name: relatedIssue
fields.description: The target issue that the source issue is related to. The relation type describes how the source issue relates to this issue.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: cursor

types.name: IssueSuggestion
types.description: [Internal] An AI-generated suggestion for an issue. Suggestions can recommend related or similar issues, assignees, labels, teams, or projects. Each suggestion has a type, state (active, accepted, dismissed), and optionally associated metadata with scoring information.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: [Internal] The issue that this suggestion applies to.

fields.name: issueId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the issue that this suggestion applies to. Can be used to query the issue directly.

fields.name: type
fields.description: [Internal] The type of suggestion, indicating what kind of entity is being suggested (e.g., similarIssue, relatedIssue, assignee, label, team, project).

fields.name: state
fields.description: [Internal] The current state of the suggestion: active, accepted, or dismissed.

fields.name: stateChangedAt
fields.description: [Internal] The date when the suggestion's state was last changed (e.g., from active to accepted or dismissed).

fields.name: dismissalReason
fields.description: [Internal] The reason the suggestion was dismissed by the user. Null if the suggestion has not been dismissed.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: [Internal] Metadata associated with the suggestion, including confidence scores and classification. Null if no metadata is available.

fields.name: suggestedIssue
fields.description: [Internal] The suggested issue, when the suggestion type is similarIssue or relatedIssue. Null for other suggestion types.

fields.name: suggestedIssueId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the suggested issue. Set when the suggestion type is similarIssue or relatedIssue. Can be used to query the issue directly.

fields.name: suggestedTeam
fields.description: [Internal] The suggested team, when the suggestion type is team. Null for other suggestion types.

fields.name: suggestedProject
fields.description: [Internal] The suggested project, when the suggestion type is project. Null for other suggestion types.

fields.name: suggestedUser
fields.description: [Internal] The suggested user, when the suggestion type is assignee. Null for other suggestion types.

fields.name: suggestedUserId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the suggested user. Set when the suggestion type is assignee. Can be used to query the user directly.

fields.name: suggestedLabel
fields.description: [Internal] The suggested label, when the suggestion type is label. Null for other suggestion types.

fields.name: suggestedLabelId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the suggested label. Set when the suggestion type is label. Can be used to query the label directly.

types.name: IssueSuggestionType
types.description: The type of an issue suggestion, indicating what kind of entity is being suggested (e.g., similar issue, assignee, label, team, project).

types.name: IssueSuggestionState
types.description: The state of an issue suggestion, indicating whether it is active, accepted, or dismissed.

types.name: IssueSuggestionMetadata
types.description: [Internal] Metadata associated with an issue suggestion, including scoring and classification information.

fields.name: score
fields.description: [Internal] The confidence score of the suggestion. Higher values indicate greater confidence. Null if no score was computed.

fields.name: classification
fields.description: [Internal] The classification category of the suggestion. Null if no classification was assigned.

fields.name: reasons
fields.description: [Internal] The reasons explaining why this suggestion was made. Null if no reasons are available.

fields.name: evalLogId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the evaluation log entry associated with this suggestion. Null if no log entry exists.

fields.name: rank
fields.description: [Internal] The rank of this suggestion relative to other suggestions of the same type. Lower values indicate higher priority. Null if not ranked.

fields.name: variant
fields.description: [Internal] The AI prompt variant that generated this suggestion. Null if not applicable.

fields.name: appliedAutomationRuleId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the automation rule that was applied from this suggestion. Null if no rule was applied.

fields.name: failedAutomationRuleId
fields.description: [Internal] Identifier of the automation rule that attempted to apply this suggestion. Null if no rule attempted to apply it or if the rule applied successfully.

fields.name: failedAutomationRuleReason
fields.description: [Internal] Reason why an automation rule could not apply this suggestion. Null if no rule attempted to apply it or if the rule applied successfully.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: IssueStateSpan
types.description: A continuous period of time during which an issue remained in a specific workflow state.

fields.name: id

fields.name: stateId
fields.description: The workflow state identifier for this span.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.description: The timestamp when the issue entered this state.

fields.name: endedAt
fields.description: The timestamp when the issue left this state. Null if the issue is currently in this state.

fields.name: state
fields.description: The workflow state for this span.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: createdAt

The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project that the initiative is associated with.

fields.name: initiative
fields.description: The initiative that the project is associated with.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The sort order of the project within its parent initiative.

types.description: A history record associated with a project. Tracks changes to project properties, status, members, teams, milestones, labels, and relationships over time.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt

fields.name: entries

fields.name: project

types.name: ProjectRelation
types.description: A dependency relation between two projects. Relations can optionally be anchored to specific milestones within each project, allowing fine-grained dependency tracking.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of dependency relationship from the project to the related project (e.g., blocks).

fields.name: project
fields.description: The source project in the dependency relation.

fields.name: projectMilestone
fields.description: The specific milestone within the source project that the relation is anchored to. Null if the relation applies to the project as a whole.

fields.name: anchorType
fields.description: The type of anchor on the source project end of the relation, indicating whether it is anchored to the project itself or a specific milestone.

fields.name: relatedProject
fields.description: The target project in the dependency relation.

fields.name: relatedProjectMilestone
fields.description: The specific milestone within the target project that the relation is anchored to. Null if the relation applies to the target project as a whole.

fields.name: relatedAnchorType
fields.description: The type of anchor on the target project end of the relation, indicating whether it is anchored to the project itself or a specific milestone.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who last created or modified the relation. Null if the user has been deleted.

types.name: TeamVisibility
types.description: The visibility of a team. A team can be public, private, or protected within an enclosing private-team boundary.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: TriageResponsibility
types.description: A team's triage responsibility configuration that defines how issues entering triage are handled. Each team can have one triage responsibility, which specifies the action to take (notify or assign) and the responsible users, determined either by a manual selection of specific users or by an on-call time schedule.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't

fields.name: action

fields.name: manualSelection
fields.description: Set of users used for triage responsibility.

fields.name: team

fields.name: timeSchedule

fields.name: currentUser

types.name: TriageResponsibilityAction
types.description: Which action should be taken after an issue is added to triage.

types.name: TriageResponsibilityManualSelection
types.description: Manual triage responsibility configuration specifying a set of users to assign triaged issues to, with optional round-robin rotation.

fields.name: userIds
fields.description: The set of users responsible for triage.

fields.name: assignmentIndex
fields.description: [Internal] The index of the current userId used for the assign action when having more than one user.

types.name: TimeSchedule
types.description: A time-based schedule defining on-call rotations or availability windows. Schedules contain a series of time entries, each specifying a user and their active period. They can be synced from external services (such as PagerDuty or Opsgenie) via integrations, or created manually. Schedules are used by triage responsibilities to determine who should be assigned or notified when issues enter triage.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the schedule.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: The schedule entries.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: The identifier of the external schedule.

fields.name: externalUrl
fields.description: The URL to the external schedule.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace of the schedule.

fields.name: integration
fields.description: The identifier of the Linear integration populating the schedule.

types.name: TimeScheduleEntry
types.description: A single entry in a time schedule, defining a time range and the user responsible during that period.

fields.name: startsAt
fields.description: The start time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.

fields.name: endsAt
fields.description: The end time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.

fields.name: userId
fields.description: The Linear user id of the user on schedule. If the user cannot be mapped to a Linear user then `userEmail` can be used as a reference.

fields.name: userEmail
fields.description: The email, name or reference to the user on schedule. This is used in case the external user could not be mapped to a Linear user id.

types.name: TeamMembership
types.description: A join entity that defines a user's membership in a team. Each membership record links a user to a team and tracks whether the user is a team owner. Users can be members of multiple teams, and their memberships determine which teams' issues and resources they can access.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that the membership is associated with.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the membership is associated with.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: Whether the user is an owner of the team. Team owners have elevated permissions for managing team settings, members, and resources.

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The sort order of this team in the user's personal team list. Lower values appear first.

types.name: GitAutomationState
types.description: A Git automation rule that automatically transitions issues to a specified workflow state when a Git event occurs (e.g., when a PR is opened, move the linked issue to 'In Review'). Each rule is scoped to a team and optionally to a specific target branch. When no target branch is specified, the rule acts as the default for all branches. Target-branch-specific rules override the defaults.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: state
fields.description: The workflow state that linked issues will be transitioned to when the Git event fires. Null if this rule is configured to take no action, overriding any default rule for the same event.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that this automation rule belongs to. Issues must belong to this team for the automation to apply.

fields.name: targetBranch
fields.description: The target branch that this automation rule applies to. When set, this rule only fires for pull requests targeting the specified branch pattern, overriding any default rule for the same event. Null if this is a default rule that applies to all branches.

fields.name: event
fields.description: The Git event that triggers this automation rule (e.g., branch created, PR opened for review, or PR merged).

fields.name: branchPattern
fields.description: [DEPRECATED] The target branch, if null, the automation will be triggered on any branch.

types.name: GitAutomationTargetBranch
types.description: A target branch definition used by Git automation rules to scope automations to specific branches. The branch can be specified as an exact name (e.g., 'main') or as a regular expression pattern (e.g., 'release/.*'). Each target branch belongs to a team and can have multiple automation rules associated with it, which override the team's default automation rules when a PR targets a matching branch.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that this target branch definition belongs to. The branch pattern is unique within a team.

fields.name: branchPattern
fields.description: The branch name or pattern to match against pull request target branches. Interpreted as a literal branch name unless isRegex is true, in which case it is treated as a regular expression.

fields.name: isRegex
fields.description: Whether the branch pattern should be interpreted as a regular expression. When false, the pattern is matched as an exact branch name.

fields.name: automationStates
fields.description: Git automation rules associated with this target branch. These rules override the team's default automation rules when a pull request targets a branch matching this pattern.

args.name: before
args.description: A cursor to be used with last for backward pagination.

args.name: after
args.description: A cursor to be used with first for forward pagination

args.name: first
args.description: The number of items to forward paginate (used with after). Defaults to 50.

args.name: last
args.description: The number of items to backward paginate (used with before). Defaults to 50.

args.name: includeArchived
args.description: Should archived resources be included (default: false)

args.name: orderBy
args.description: By which field should the pagination order by. Available options are createdAt (default) and updatedAt.

types.name: GitAutomationStates
types.description: The Git events that can trigger an automation rule. Each value corresponds to a pull/merge request lifecycle event (e.g., branch created, PR opened for review, PR merged).

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: Webhook
types.description: A webhook subscription that sends HTTP POST callbacks to an external URL when events occur in Linear. Webhooks can be scoped to a specific team, multiple teams, or all public teams in the organization. They support filtering by resource type (e.g., Issue, Comment, Project) and can be created either manually by users or automatically by OAuth applications. Each webhook has a signing secret for verifying payload authenticity on the recipient side.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: label
fields.description: A human-readable label for the webhook, used for identification in the UI. Null if no label has been set.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The destination URL where webhook payloads will be sent via HTTP POST. Null for OAuth application webhooks, which use the webhook URL configured on the OAuth client instead.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the webhook is enabled. When disabled, no payloads will be delivered even if matching events occur.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The single team that the webhook is scoped to. When null, the webhook either targets all public teams (if allPublicTeams is true), multiple specific teams (if teamIds is set), or organization-wide events.

fields.name: teamIds
fields.description: [INTERNAL] An array of team IDs that the webhook is subscribed to. Used to represent a webhook that targets multiple specific teams, potentially in addition to all public teams (when allPublicTeams is also true). Null when the webhook targets a single team via teamId or all public teams only.

fields.name: allPublicTeams
fields.description: Whether the webhook receives events from all public (non-private) teams in the organization, including teams created after the webhook was set up. When true, the webhook automatically covers new public teams without requiring reconfiguration.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the webhook.

fields.name: secret
fields.description: A secret token used to sign webhook payloads with HMAC-SHA256, allowing the recipient to verify that the payload originated from Linear and was not tampered with. Automatically generated if not provided during creation.

fields.name: resourceTypes
fields.description: The resource types this webhook is subscribed to (e.g., 'Issue', 'Comment', 'Project', 'Cycle'). The webhook will only receive payloads for events affecting these resource types.

fields.name: failures
fields.description: [INTERNAL] Webhook failure events associated with the webhook (last 50).

types.name: WebhookFailureEvent
types.description: A record of a failed webhook delivery attempt. Created when a webhook payload could not be successfully delivered to the destination URL, either due to an HTTP error response or a network failure. Each failure event captures the HTTP status code (if available), the response body or error message, and a stable execution ID that is shared across retries of the same delivery.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description: The webhook that this failure event is associated with.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL that the webhook was trying to push to.

fields.name: httpStatus
fields.description: The HTTP status code returned by the webhook recipient. Null if the request failed before receiving a response (e.g., DNS resolution failure, connection timeout).

fields.name: responseOrError
fields.description: The HTTP response body returned by the recipient, or the error message if the request failed before receiving a response. Truncated to 1000 characters.

fields.name: executionId
fields.description: A stable identifier for the webhook delivery attempt. This ID remains the same across retries of the same payload, making it useful for correlating multiple failure events that belong to the same logical delivery.

types.name: OrganizationIpRestriction
types.description: [INTERNAL] IP restriction rule for a workspace, defining allowed or blocked IP ranges.

fields.name: range
fields.description: IP range in CIDR format.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Restriction type.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional restriction description.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the restriction is enabled.

types.name: ReleaseChannel
types.description: Features release channel.

types.name: SLADayCountType
types.description: Which day count to use for SLA calculations.

types.name: ProjectUpdateReminderFrequency
types.description: The frequency at which to send project update reminders.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: PaidSubscription
types.description: The billing subscription of a workspace. Represents an active paid plan (e.g., Basic, Business, Enterprise) backed by a Stripe subscription. If a workspace has no Subscription record, it is on the free plan. Only one active subscription per workspace is expected.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The subscription plan type (e.g., basic, business, enterprise). Determines the feature set and pricing tier for the workspace.

fields.name: seats
fields.description: The number of seats (active members) in the subscription. This is the raw count before applying minimum and maximum seat limits.

fields.name: seatsMinimum
fields.description: The minimum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription. The billed seat count will never go below this value even if actual member count is lower. Null if no minimum is enforced.

fields.name: seatsMaximum
fields.description: The maximum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription. The billed seat count will never exceed this value even if actual member count is higher. Null if no maximum is enforced.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who initially created (purchased) the subscription. Null if the creator has been removed from the workspace.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the subscription is associated with.

fields.name: collectionMethod
fields.description: The billing collection method for this subscription. 'automatic' means the payment method on file is charged automatically. 'send_invoice' means invoices are sent to the billing email for manual payment.

fields.name: canceledAt
fields.description: The date the subscription was canceled. Null if the subscription has not been canceled.

fields.name: cancelAt
fields.description: The date the subscription is scheduled to be canceled in the future. Null if no cancellation is scheduled. The subscription remains active until this date.

fields.name: pendingChangeType
fields.description: The subscription plan type that the workspace is scheduled to change to at the next billing cycle. Null if no plan change is pending.

fields.name: nextBillingAt
fields.description: The date the subscription will be billed next. Null if the subscription is canceled or has no upcoming billing date.

types.name: IdentityProvider
types.description: A SAML/SSO or web forms identity provider configuration for a workspace. Identity providers enable single sign-on authentication via SAML 2.0 and can optionally support SCIM for automated user provisioning and group-based role mapping. Each workspace can have a default general-purpose identity provider and additional web forms identity providers for Asks web authentication.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: defaultMigrated
fields.description: Whether the identity provider is the default identity provider migrated from organization level settings.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of identity provider.

fields.name: samlEnabled
fields.description: Whether SAML authentication is enabled for the workspace.

fields.name: ssoEndpoint
fields.description: Sign in endpoint URL for the identity provider.

fields.name: ssoBinding
fields.description: Binding method for authentication call. Can be either `post` (default) or `redirect`.

fields.name: ssoSignAlgo
fields.description: The algorithm of the Signing Certificate. Can be one of `sha1`, `sha256` (default), or `sha512`.

fields.name: ssoSigningCert
fields.description: X.509 Signing Certificate in string form.

fields.name: issuerEntityId
fields.description: The issuer's custom entity ID.

fields.name: spEntityId
fields.description: The service provider (Linear) custom entity ID. Defaults to https://auth.linear.app/sso

fields.name: priority
fields.description: The SAML priority used to pick default workspace in SAML SP initiated flow, when same domain is claimed for SAML by multiple workspaces. Lower priority value means higher preference.

fields.name: scimEnabled
fields.description: Whether SCIM provisioning is enabled for the workspace.

fields.name: ownersGroupPush
fields.description: [INTERNAL] SCIM owners group push settings.

fields.name: adminsGroupPush
fields.description: [INTERNAL] SCIM admins group push settings.

fields.name: guestsGroupPush
fields.description: [INTERNAL] SCIM guests group push settings.

fields.name: allowNameChange
fields.description: Whether users are allowed to change their name and display name even if SCIM is enabled.

types.name: IdentityProviderType
types.description: The type of identity provider.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: IssueDraft
types.description: [Internal] A draft issue that has not yet been created as a full issue. Drafts allow users to prepare issue details (title, description, labels, assignee, etc.) before committing them. Drafts belong to a team and a creator, and support a parent-child hierarchy similar to issues. A draft can have either a parent draft or a parent issue, but not both.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The draft's title. This will become the issue's title when the draft is published.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The draft's description in markdown format.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: The priority of the draft. 0 = No priority, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Normal, 4 = Low.

fields.name: estimate
fields.description: The estimate of the complexity of the draft. Null if no estimate has been set.

fields.name: dueDate
fields.description: The date at which the issue would be due.

fields.name: labelIds
fields.description: Identifiers of the labels added to the draft. These labels will be applied to the issue when the draft is published.

fields.name: teamId
fields.description: Identifier of the team associated with the draft. Can be used to query the team directly.

fields.name: cycleId
fields.description: Identifier of the cycle associated with the draft. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if no cycle is assigned.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Identifier of the project associated with the draft. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if no project is assigned.

fields.name: projectMilestoneId
fields.description: Identifier of the project milestone associated with the draft. Can be used to query the project milestone directly. Null if no milestone is assigned.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the draft.

fields.name: assigneeId
fields.description: Identifier of the user assigned to the draft. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the draft is unassigned.

fields.name: delegateId
fields.description: The agent user delegated to work on the issue being drafted.

fields.name: stateId
fields.description: Identifier of the workflow state associated with the draft. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Determines the initial status the issue will have when the draft is published.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent draft of the draft. Set when this draft represents a sub-issue of another draft. Null if this is a top-level draft or has a parent issue instead.

fields.name: parentId
fields.description: The ID of the parent issue draft, if any.

fields.name: sourceCommentId
fields.description: Identifier of the comment that the draft was created from. Set when a draft is created from an existing comment. Null if the draft was not created from a comment.

fields.name: parentIssue
fields.description: The parent issue of the draft. Set when this draft represents a sub-issue that will be created under an existing issue. Null if this is a top-level draft or has a parent draft instead.

fields.name: parentIssueId
fields.description: The ID of the parent issue, if any.

fields.name: subIssueSortOrder
fields.description: The order of items in the sub-draft list. Only set if the draft has `parent` set.

fields.name: priorityLabel
fields.description: Label for the priority.

fields.name: descriptionData
fields.description: [Internal] The draft's description as a Prosemirror document.

fields.name: attachments
fields.description: Serialized array of JSONs representing attachments.

fields.name: needs
fields.description: Serialized array of JSONs representing customer needs.

fields.name: releaseIds
fields.description: Identifiers of the releases associated with the draft. These releases will be linked to the issue when the draft is published.

fields.name: schedule
fields.description: Serialized array of JSONs representing the recurring issue's schedule.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: Draft
types.description: A general-purpose draft for unsaved content. Drafts store in-progress text for comments, project updates, initiative updates, posts, pull request comments, and customer needs. Each draft belongs to a user and is associated with exactly one parent entity. Drafts are automatically deleted when the user publishes the corresponding comment or update.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: bodyData
fields.description: The draft text content as a ProseMirror document.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional properties for the draft, such as generation metadata for AI-generated drafts, health status for project updates, or post titles.

fields.name: isAutogenerated
fields.description: Whether the draft was autogenerated for the user.

fields.name: wasLocalDraft
fields.description: [INTERNAL] Whether the draft was ported from a local draft.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who created the draft.

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue for which this is a draft comment. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project for which this is a draft comment or project update. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: projectUpdate
fields.description: The project update for which this is a draft comment. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: initiative
fields.description: The initiative for which this is a draft comment or initiative update. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: initiativeUpdate
fields.description: The initiative update for which this is a draft comment. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: post
fields.description: The post for which this is a draft comment. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: parentComment
fields.description: The parent comment for which this is a draft reply. Null if the draft is a top-level comment or belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: customerNeed
fields.description: The customer need that this draft is referencing. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: anchor
fields.description: [INTERNAL] Allows for multiple drafts per entity (currently constrained to Pull Requests).

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team for which this is a draft post. Null if the draft belongs to a different parent entity type.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

types.name: NotificationCategory
types.description: The categories of notifications a user can subscribe to.

types.name: NotificationSubscription
types.description: A subscription that controls which notifications a user receives for a specific entity such as a team, project, cycle, label, custom view, initiative, or user. This is not a billing subscription -- it determines notification preferences. Each subscription is scoped to exactly one target entity and specifies the notification types the subscriber wants to receive. When active, matching events on the target entity generate notifications for the subscriber.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: subscriber
fields.description: The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.

fields.name: customer
fields.description: The customer that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: customView
fields.description: The custom view that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: cycle
fields.description: The cycle that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: label
fields.description: The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: initiative
fields.description: The initiative that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that this notification subscription is scoped to, for user-specific view subscriptions. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.

fields.name: contextViewType
fields.description: The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a specific view type.

fields.name: userContextViewType
fields.description: The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.

fields.name: active
fields.description: Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.

types.name: ProjectNotification
types.description: A notification related to a project, such as being added as a member or lead, project updates, comments, or mentions on the project or its milestones.

fields.name: commentId
fields.description: Related comment ID. Null if the notification is not related to a comment.

fields.name: parentCommentId
fields.description: Related parent comment ID. Null if the notification is not related to a comment.

fields.name: reactionEmoji
fields.description: Name of the reaction emoji related to the notification.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Notification type. Determines the kind of event that triggered this notification and which associated entity fields will be populated.

fields.name: actor
fields.description: The user that caused the notification. Null if the notification was triggered by a non-user actor such as an integration, external user, or system event.

fields.name: externalUserActor
fields.description: The external user that caused the notification. Populated when the notification was triggered by an external user (e.g., a commenter from a connected integration like Slack or GitHub) rather than a Linear workspace member.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The recipient user of this notification.

fields.name: readAt
fields.description: The time at which the user marked the notification as read. Null if the notification is unread.

fields.name: emailedAt
fields.description: The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.

fields.name: snoozedUntilAt

types.name: OauthClientApproval
types.description: A request to install an OAuth client application on a workspace, along with the admin's approval or denial response. When a user attempts to install an OAuth application that requires admin approval, an approval record is created. A workspace admin can then approve or deny the request, optionally providing a reason. The record also tracks any newly requested scopes that were added after the initial approval.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: oauthClientId
fields.description: The identifier of the OAuth client application being requested for installation in this workspace.

fields.name: requesterId
fields.description: The identifier of the user who initiated the request to install the OAuth client application.

fields.name: responderId
fields.description: The identifier of the workspace admin who approved or denied the installation request. Null if the request has not yet been responded to.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the approval request: requested (pending admin review), approved, or denied.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: The OAuth scopes that the application has been approved to use within this workspace (e.g., 'read', 'write', 'issues:create').

fields.name: requestReason
fields.description: An optional message from the requester explaining why they want to install the OAuth application.

fields.name: denyReason
fields.description: An optional explanation from the admin for why the installation request was denied.

fields.name: newlyRequestedScopes
fields.description: Additional OAuth scopes requested after the initial approval. These scopes are not yet approved and require a separate admin decision. Null if no additional scopes have been requested. These scopes will never overlap with the already-approved scopes.

types.name: OAuthClientApprovalStatus
types.description: The different requests statuses possible for an OAuth client approval request.
