types.name: AuditEntry
types.description: A workspace audit log entry recording a security or compliance-relevant action. Audit entries capture who performed an action, when, from what IP address and country, and include type-specific metadata. The audit log is partitioned by time for performance and is accessible only to workspace administrators. Examples of audited actions include user authentication events, permission changes, data exports, and workspace setting modifications.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of audited action (e.g., user authentication, permission change, data export, setting modification).

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace the audit log belongs to.

fields.name: actor
fields.description: The user that caused the audit entry to be created.

fields.name: actorId
fields.description: The ID of the user that caused the audit entry to be created.

fields.name: ip
fields.description: The IP address of the actor at the time the audited action was performed. Null if the IP was not captured.

fields.name: countryCode
fields.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code derived from the request IP address. Null if geo-location could not be determined.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Additional metadata related to the audit entry.

fields.name: requestInformation
fields.description: Additional information related to the request which performed the action.

types.name: EmailIntakeAddress
types.description: An email address that creates Linear issues when emails are sent to it. Email intake addresses can be scoped to a specific team or issue template, and support configurable auto-reply messages for issue creation, completion, and cancellation events. They can also be configured for the Asks web form feature, enabling external users to submit requests via email.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The unique local part (before the @) of the intake email address, used to route incoming emails to the correct intake handler.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the email address.

fields.name: forwardingEmailAddress
fields.description: The email address used to forward emails to the intake address.

fields.name: senderName
fields.description: The name to be used for outgoing emails.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the email address is enabled.

fields.name: repliesEnabled
fields.description: Whether email replies are enabled.

fields.name: useUserNamesInReplies
fields.description: Whether the commenter's name is included in the email replies.

fields.name: template
fields.description: The template that the email address is associated with.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the email address is associated with.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The workspace that the email address is associated with.

fields.name: sesDomainIdentity
fields.description: The SES domain identity that the email address is associated with.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the email intake address.

fields.name: customerRequestsEnabled
fields.description: Whether issues created from emails sent to this address are automatically converted into customer requests, linking the sender as a customer contact.

fields.name: issueCreatedAutoReply
fields.description: The auto-reply message for issue created. If not set, the default reply will be used.

fields.name: issueCreatedAutoReplyEnabled
fields.description: Whether the auto-reply for issue created is enabled.

fields.name: issueCompletedAutoReplyEnabled
fields.description: Whether the auto-reply for issue completed is enabled.

fields.name: issueCompletedAutoReply
fields.description: The auto-reply message for issue completed. If not set, the default reply will be used.

fields.name: issueCanceledAutoReplyEnabled
fields.description: Whether the auto-reply for issue canceled is enabled.

fields.name: issueCanceledAutoReply
fields.description: The auto-reply message for issue canceled. If not set, the default reply will be used.

fields.name: reopenOnReply
fields.description: Whether to reopen completed or canceled issues when a substantive email reply is received.

types.name: SesDomainIdentity
types.description: A verified Amazon SES domain identity that enables sending emails from a custom domain. Organizations configure SES domain identities to send issue notification replies and Asks auto-replies from their own domain instead of the default Linear domain. Full verification requires DKIM signing, a custom MAIL FROM domain, and organization ownership confirmation. Multiple organizations sharing the same domain each have their own SesDomainIdentity record but share the underlying SES identity.

types.name: IntegrationTemplate
types.description: A connection between a template and an integration. This join entity links Linear issue templates to integrations so that external systems (e.g., Slack Asks channels) can use specific templates when creating issues. Each record optionally includes a foreign entity ID to scope the template to a specific external resource, such as a particular Slack channel.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: template
fields.description: The template that the integration is associated with.

fields.name: integration
fields.description: The integration that the template is associated with.

fields.name: foreignEntityId
fields.description: The identifier of the foreign entity in the external service that this template is scoped to. For example, a Slack channel ID indicating which channel should use this template for creating issues. When null, the template applies to the integration as a whole rather than a specific external resource.

fields.description: The user who leads the project. The project lead is typically responsible for posting status updates and driving the project to completion. Null if no lead is assigned.

fields.description: The resolution of the project's estimated completion date, indicating whether it refers to a specific month, quarter, half-year, or year.

fields.name: canceledAt
fields.description: The time at which the project was moved into a canceled status. Null if the project has not been canceled.

fields.description: The time at which the project was automatically archived by the auto-pruning process. Null if the project has not been auto-archived.

fields.description: The sort order for the project within the workspace when ordered by priority.

fields.description: The issue that was converted into this project. Null if the project was not created from an issue.

fields.name: lastAppliedTemplate
fields.description: The last template that was applied to this project.

fields.description: The priority of the project. 0 = No priority, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Medium, 4 = Low.

fields.name: lastUpdate
fields.description: The most recent status update posted for this project. Null if no updates have been posted.

fields.name: health
fields.description: The overall health of the project, derived from the most recent project update. Possible values are onTrack, atRisk, or offTrack. Null if no health has been reported.

fields.description: The time at which the project health was last updated, typically when a new project update is posted. Null if health has never been set.

fields.name: state
fields.description: [DEPRECATED] The type of the state.

fields.name: priorityLabel
fields.description: The priority of the project as a label.

fields.name: syncedWith
fields.description: The external services the project is synced with.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Metadata related to search result.

fields.name: archivePayload
fields.description: Archived entities matching the search term along with all their dependencies, serialized for the client sync engine.

fields.name: totalCount
fields.description: Total number of matching results before pagination is applied.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Used in `before` and `after` args

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: number
fields.description: The issue's unique number, scoped to the issue's team. Together with the team key, this forms the issue's human-readable identifier (e.g., ENG-123).

fields.name: title
fields.description: The issue's title. This is the primary human-readable summary of the work item.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: The priority of the issue. 0 = No priority, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Medium, 4 = Low.

fields.name: estimate
fields.description: The estimate of the complexity of the issue. The specific scale used depends on the team's estimation configuration (e.g., points, T-shirt sizes). Null if no estimate has been set.

fields.name: boardOrder
fields.description: The order of the item in its column on the board.
fields.deprecationReason: Will be removed in near future, please use `sortOrder` instead

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: The order of the item in relation to other items in the organization. Used for manual sorting in list views.

fields.name: prioritySortOrder
fields.description: The order of the item in relation to other items in the workspace, when ordered by priority.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was moved into started state.

fields.name: completedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was moved into completed state.

fields.name: startedTriageAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue entered triage.

fields.name: triagedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue left triage.

fields.name: canceledAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was moved into canceled state.

fields.name: autoClosedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was automatically closed by the auto pruning process.

fields.name: autoArchivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was automatically archived by the auto pruning process.

fields.name: dueDate
fields.description: The date at which the issue is due.

fields.name: slaStartedAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue's SLA began.

fields.name: slaMediumRiskAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue's SLA will enter medium risk state.

fields.name: slaHighRiskAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue's SLA will enter high risk state.

fields.name: slaBreachesAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue's SLA will breach.

fields.name: slaType
fields.description: The type of SLA set on the issue. Calendar days or business days.

fields.name: addedToProjectAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was added to a project.

fields.name: addedToCycleAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was added to a cycle.

fields.name: addedToTeamAt
fields.description: The time at which the issue was added to a team.

fields.name: trashed
fields.description: A flag that indicates whether the issue is in the trash bin.

fields.name: snoozedUntilAt
fields.description: The time until an issue will be snoozed in Triage view.

fields.name: suggestionsGeneratedAt
fields.description: [Internal] The time at which the most recent suggestions for this issue were generated.

fields.name: activitySummary
fields.description: [Internal] The activity summary information for this issue.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: [Internal] AI-generated activity summary for this issue.

fields.name: labelIds
fields.description: Identifiers of the labels associated with this issue. Can be used to query the labels directly.

fields.name: team
fields.description: The team that the issue belongs to. Every issue must belong to exactly one team, which determines the available workflow states, labels, and other team-specific configuration.

fields.name: cycle
fields.description: The cycle that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not part of any cycle.

fields.name: project
fields.description: The project that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not part of any project.

fields.name: projectMilestone
fields.description: The project milestone that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not assigned to a specific milestone within its project.

fields.name: lastAppliedTemplate
fields.description: The last template that was applied to this issue.

fields.name: recurringIssueTemplate
fields.description: The recurring issue template that created this issue.

fields.name: previousIdentifiers
fields.description: Previous identifiers of the issue if it has been moved between teams.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who created the issue. Null if the creator's account has been deleted or if the issue was created by an integration or system process.

fields.name: externalUserCreator
fields.description: The external user who created the issue. Set when the issue was created via an integration (e.g., Slack, Intercom) on behalf of a non-Linear user. Null if the issue was created by a Linear user.

fields.name: assignee
fields.description: The user to whom the issue is assigned. Null if the issue is unassigned.

fields.name: delegate
fields.description: The agent user that is delegated to work on this issue. Set when an AI agent has been assigned to perform work on this issue. Null if no agent is working on the issue.

fields.name: snoozedBy
fields.description: The user who snoozed the issue.

fields.name: state
fields.description: The workflow state (issue status) that the issue is currently in. Workflow states represent the issue's progress through the team's workflow, such as Triage, Todo, In Progress, Done, or Canceled.

fields.name: subIssueSortOrder
fields.description: The order of the item in the sub-issue list. Only set if the issue has a parent.

fields.name: reactionData
fields.description: Emoji reaction summary for the issue, grouped by emoji type. Contains the count and reacting user information for each emoji.

fields.name: priorityLabel
fields.description: Label for the priority.

fields.name: sourceComment
fields.description: The comment that this issue was created from, when an issue is created from an existing comment. Null if the issue was not created from a comment.

fields.name: integrationSourceType
fields.description: Integration type that created this issue, if applicable.

fields.name: inheritsSharedAccess
fields.description: Whether this issue inherits shared access from its parent issue.

fields.name: documents
fields.description: Documents associated with the issue.

types.name: UserSettings
types.description: Per-user settings and preferences for a workspace member. Includes notification delivery preferences, email subscription settings, notification category and channel preferences, theme configuration, and various UI preferences. Each user has exactly one UserSettings record per workspace.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: notificationDeliveryPreferences
fields.description: The notification delivery preferences for the user. Note: notificationDisabled field is deprecated in favor of notificationChannelPreferences.

fields.name: unsubscribedFrom
fields.description: The email types the user has unsubscribed from.
fields.deprecationReason: Use individual subscription fields instead. This field's value is now outdated.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that these settings belong to.

fields.name: calendarHash
fields.description: A unique hash for the user, used to construct secure calendar subscription URLs.

fields.name: subscribedToChangelog
fields.description: Whether this user is subscribed to receive changelog emails about Linear product updates.

fields.name: subscribedToDPA
fields.description: Whether this user is subscribed to receive Data Processing Agreement (DPA) related emails.

fields.name: subscribedToInviteAccepted
fields.description: Whether this user is subscribed to receive email notifications when their workspace invitations are accepted.

fields.name: subscribedToPrivacyLegalUpdates
fields.description: Whether this user is subscribed to receive emails about privacy policy and legal updates.

fields.name: feedSummarySchedule
fields.description: The user's preferred schedule for receiving feed summary digests. Null if the user has not set a preference and will use the workspace default.

fields.name: showFullUserNames
fields.description: Whether to show full user names instead of display names.

fields.name: feedLastSeenTime
fields.description: The user's last seen time for the pulse feed.

fields.name: autoAssignToSelf
fields.description: Whether to auto-assign newly created issues to the current user by default.

fields.name: notificationCategoryPreferences
fields.description: The user's notification category preferences, indicating which notification categories are enabled or disabled per notification channel.

fields.name: notificationChannelPreferences
fields.description: The user's notification channel preferences, indicating which notification delivery channels (email, in-app, mobile push, Slack) are enabled.

fields.name: theme
fields.description: The user's theme configuration for the specified color mode (light/dark) and device type (desktop/mobile). Returns null if no valid theme preset is configured.

args.name: deviceType
args.description: The device type.

args.name: mode
args.description: The theme color mode.

types.description: Delivery preferences for a specific notification channel, including an optional delivery schedule that restricts when notifications are sent.

fields.deprecationReason: This field has been replaced by notificationChannelPreferences

types.name: NotificationDeliveryPreferencesSchedule
types.description: A user's weekly notification delivery schedule, defining delivery windows for each day of the week. Notifications outside these windows are held and delivered when the window opens.

fields.name: disabled
fields.description: Whether the entire delivery schedule is disabled. When true, notifications are delivered at any time regardless of the per-day settings.

fields.description: Delivery preferences for Sunday.

fields.description: Delivery preferences for Friday.

fields.name: saturday
fields.description: Delivery preferences for Saturday.

types.name: NotificationDeliveryPreferencesDay
types.description: A user's notification delivery window for a specific day of the week. Defines the time range during which notifications will be delivered.

fields.name: start
fields.description: The start time of the notification delivery window in HH:MM military time format (e.g., '09:00'). Must be earlier than 'end'.

fields.name: end
fields.description: The end time of the notification delivery window in HH:MM military time format (e.g., '18:00'). Must be later than 'start'.

fields.name: attachmentLinkURL
fields.description: Link any URL to an issue. If the workspace has a matching integration configured and the URL is recognized (e.g., Zendesk, GitHub, Slack), a rich attachment will be created that enables features like automated status updates. Otherwise, a basic attachment is created.

args.name: createAsUser
args.description: Create attachment as a user with the provided name. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating attachments in `actor=app` mode.

args.name: displayIconUrl
args.description: Provide an external user avatar URL. Can only be used in conjunction with the `createAsUser` options. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating comments in `actor=app` mode.

args.name: title
args.description: The title to use for the attachment.

args.name: url
args.description: The url to link.

args.name: issueId
args.description: The issue for which to link the url. Can be a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123').

args.name: id
args.description: The id for the attachment.

args.name: issueId

args.description: The URL of the GitLab merge request to link.

fields.name: releasePipelineCreate
fields.description: Creates a new release pipeline with default stages. Subject to plan entitlement and quota limits.

args.name: input
args.description: The ReleasePipeline object to create.

fields.name: releasePipelineUpdate
fields.description: Updates an existing release pipeline. Supports updating name, slug, type, production flag, path patterns, and team associations. Private teams that the current user cannot access are preserved in the team list.

args.name: input
args.description: A partial ReleasePipeline object to update the ReleasePipeline with.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release pipeline to update.

fields.name: releasePipelineArchive
fields.description: Archives a release pipeline.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release pipeline to archive.

fields.name: releasePipelineUnarchive
fields.description: Unarchives a release pipeline.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release pipeline to unarchive.

fields.name: releasePipelineDelete
fields.description: Permanently deletes a release pipeline and all associated stages and releases.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release pipeline to delete.

fields.name: releaseSync
fields.description: Syncs release data by resolving issue and pull request references and associating them with a release. For continuous pipelines, creates a new completed release. For scheduled pipelines, finds or creates a started release and accumulates issues into it.

args.name: input
args.description: The release data to sync.

fields.name: releaseCreate
fields.description: Creates a new release in a pipeline. If no stage is specified, defaults to the first completed stage for continuous pipelines or the first started stage for scheduled pipelines.

args.name: input
args.description: The release to create.

fields.name: releaseUpdate
fields.description: Updates an existing release by ID. Supports updating name, description, version, commit SHA, pipeline, stage, and dates.

args.name: input
args.description: A partial Release object to update the Release with.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release to update.

fields.name: releaseComplete
fields.description: Marks a release as completed. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release.

args.name: input
args.description: The input containing the pipeline ID and optional version.

fields.name: releaseUpdateByPipeline
fields.description: Updates a release by pipeline identifier. Finds the release by version or latest started/planned release, and optionally transitions it to a new stage by name.

args.name: input
args.description: The input containing the pipeline ID, optional version, and optional stage name.

fields.name: releaseDelete
fields.description: Moves a release to the trash bin. Trashed releases are archived and will be permanently deleted after a retention period. If the release is already archived, it is marked as trashed with a fresh archive timestamp.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release to delete.

fields.name: releaseArchive
fields.description: Archives a release.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release to archive.

fields.name: releaseUnarchive
fields.description: Unarchives a release.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release to unarchive.

fields.name: releaseSyncByAccessKey
fields.description: Syncs release data using an access key for CI/CD integration. The pipeline is automatically inferred from the access key's configured resources, so no pipeline ID is needed in the input.

args.name: input
args.description: The release data to sync.

fields.name: releaseCompleteByAccessKey
fields.description: Marks a release as completed using an access key. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release. The pipeline is inferred from the access key.

args.name: input
args.description: The input containing the optional version.

fields.name: releaseUpdateByPipelineByAccessKey
fields.description: Updates a release by pipeline using an access key.

args.name: input
args.description: The input containing the optional version and optional stage name.

fields.name: releaseStageCreate
fields.description: Creates a new release stage in a pipeline. Non-started stages must use default names and colors, and only one stage of each non-started type is allowed per pipeline. Started stages can optionally be frozen, but at least one non-frozen started stage must remain.

args.name: input
args.description: The ReleaseStage object to create.

fields.name: releaseStageUpdate
fields.description: Updates an existing release stage. Only started-type stages can be edited. Supports updating name, color, position, and frozen status.

args.name: input
args.description: A partial ReleaseStage object to update the ReleaseStage with.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release stage to update.

fields.name: releaseStageArchive
fields.description: Archives a release stage. Only started-type stages can be archived, and only if they have no active releases and at least one other stage of the same type remains. Cannot archive the last non-frozen started stage.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release stage to archive.

fields.name: releaseStageUnarchive
fields.description: Unarchives a release stage.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the release stage to unarchive.

args.description: The identifier of the team membership to delete.

args.name: input
args.description: A partial user object to update the user with.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user to update. Use `me` to reference currently authenticated user.

args.description: The Discord OAuth redirect URI.

args.name: code
args.description: The Discord OAuth code.

fields.description: Disconnects the external user from this Linear account.

args.name: service
args.description: The external service to disconnect.

fields.description: Changes the workspace role of a user. The requesting user must have a role equal to or higher than the target role. Requires workspace admin permissions.

args.name: role
args.description: The new role for the user.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user

fields.name: userSuspend
fields.description: Suspends a user, deactivating their account and revoking access to the workspace. The suspended user's sessions are invalidated. Can only be called by a workspace admin or owner.

args.name: forceBypassScimRestrictions
args.description: [INTERNAL] Whether to bypass SCIM restrictions when suspending. Use with caution — this overrides identity provider management.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user to suspend.

fields.name: userRevokeAllSessions
fields.description: Revokes all active sessions for a user, forcing them to re-authenticate. Can only be called by a workspace admin or owner.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user to logout all sessions of.

fields.name: userRevokeSession
fields.description: Revokes a specific authentication session for a user. The admin cannot revoke their own sessions with this mutation. Can only be called by a workspace admin or owner.

args.name: sessionId
args.description: The identifier of the session to revoke.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user whose session to revoke.

fields.name: userUnsuspend
fields.description: Re-activates a suspended user, restoring their access to the workspace. Can only be called by a workspace admin or owner.

args.name: forceBypassScimRestrictions
args.description: [INTERNAL] Whether to bypass SCIM restrictions when unsuspending. Use with caution — this overrides identity provider management.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the user to unsuspend.

fields.name: userUnlinkFromIdentityProvider
fields.description: Unlinks a guest user from their identity provider. Can only be called by an admin when SCIM is enabled.

args.name: id
args.description: The identifier of the guest user to unlink from their identity provider.

args.name: flags
args.description: The flags to reset. If not provided all flags will be reset.

fields.name: userFlagUpdate
fields.description: Updates a specific user settings flag by performing an operation (e.g., increment or clear) on it.

args.name: operation
args.description: Flag operation to perform.

args.name: flag

The content payload of the agent activity. This object is not strictly typed.
See https://linear.app/developers/agent-interaction#activity-content-payload for typing details.

inputFields.description: Whether the activity is ephemeral, and should disappear after the next activity. Defaults to false.

inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: issueId
inputFields.description: The issue that this session will be associated with. Can be a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123').

types.name: AgentSessionUpdateInput
types.description: Input for updating an agent session.

inputFields.name: externalLink
inputFields.description: The URL of an external agent-hosted page associated with this session. Only updatable by the OAuth application that owns the session.

inputFields.name: externalUrls
inputFields.description: URLs of external resources associated with this session. Replaces existing URLs. Only updatable by the OAuth application that owns the session. If supplied, addedExternalUrls and removedExternalUrls are ignored.

inputFields.name: addedExternalUrls
inputFields.description: URLs of external resources to be added to this session. Only updatable by the OAuth application that owns the session.

inputFields.name: removedExternalUrls
inputFields.description: URLs to be removed from this session. Only updatable by the OAuth application that owns the session.

inputFields.name: plan
inputFields.description: A dynamically updated list of the agent's execution strategy. Only updatable by the OAuth application that owns the session.

inputFields.name: dismissedAt
inputFields.description: [Internal] The time at which the agent session was dismissed. Set to null to un-dismiss. Only updatable by internal clients.

inputFields.name: userState
inputFields.description: [Internal] User-specific state for the agent session. Only updatable by internal clients.

inputFields.name: userId
inputFields.description: The ID of the user this state belongs to.

inputFields.name: lastReadAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the user most recently viewed the session.

types.name: AttachmentCreateInput
types.description: Input for creating a new issue attachment.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: The attachment title.

inputFields.name: subtitle
inputFields.description: The attachment subtitle.

inputFields.name: url
inputFields.description: Attachment location which is also used as an unique identifier for the attachment. If another attachment is created with the same `url` value, existing record is updated instead.

inputFields.name: issueId
inputFields.description: The issue to associate the attachment with. Can be a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123').

inputFields.name: iconUrl
inputFields.description: An icon url to display with the attachment. Should be of jpg or png format. Maximum of 1MB in size. Dimensions should be 20x20px for optimal display quality.

inputFields.name: metadata
inputFields.description: Attachment metadata object with string and number values.

inputFields.name: groupBySource
inputFields.description: Indicates if attachments for the same source application should be grouped in the Linear UI.

inputFields.name: commentBody
inputFields.description: Create a linked comment with markdown body.

inputFields.name: commentBodyData
inputFields.description: [Internal] Create a linked comment with Prosemirror body. Please use `commentBody` instead.

inputFields.name: createAsUser
inputFields.description: Create attachment as a user with the provided name. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating attachments in `actor=application` mode.

inputFields.name: displayIconUrl
inputFields.description: Provide an external user avatar URL. Can only be used in conjunction with the `createAsUser` options. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating attachments in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.description: [Internal] The comment content as a Prosemirror document.

inputFields.description: The issue to associate the comment with. Can be a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123').

inputFields.description: The initiative update to associate the comment with.

inputFields.description: Create comment as a user with the provided name. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating comments in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: displayIconUrl
inputFields.description: Provide an external user avatar URL. Can only be used in conjunction with the `createAsUser` options. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating comments in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the comment was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.

inputFields.name: doNotSubscribeToIssue

inputFields.name: createOnSyncedSlackThread
inputFields.description: Flag to indicate this comment should be created on the issue's synced Slack comment thread. If no synced Slack comment thread exists, the mutation will fail. If there are multiple synced Slack threads on the issue, the oldest one will be targeted.

inputFields.name: quotedText
inputFields.description: The text that this comment references. Only defined for inline comments.

inputFields.name: subscriberIds

types.name: CustomerNeedPayload
types.description: Return type for customer need mutations.

fields.name: lastSyncId
fields.description: The identifier of the last sync operation.

fields.name: need
fields.description: The customer need entity that was created or updated by the mutation.

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the operation was successful.

types.name: CustomerNeedCreateInput
types.description: Input for creating a customer need linked to an issue or project. Either issueId or projectId must be provided.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: customerId
inputFields.description: The UUID of the customer this need belongs to. Cannot be used together with customerExternalId.

inputFields.name: customerExternalId
inputFields.description: The external system ID of the customer this need belongs to. Cannot be used together with customerId.

inputFields.name: issueId
inputFields.description: The issue to link this need to. Accepts a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123'). Either issueId or projectId must be provided.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: [INTERNAL] The project to link this need to. Either issueId or projectId must be provided.

inputFields.name: commentId
inputFields.description: The UUID of an existing comment to associate with this need for additional context.

inputFields.name: attachmentId
inputFields.description: The UUID of an existing attachment to associate with this need as its source.

inputFields.name: priority
inputFields.description: Whether the customer need is important or not. 0 = Not important, 1 = Important.

inputFields.name: body
inputFields.description: The body content of the need in Markdown format. Cannot be used together with bodyData.

inputFields.name: bodyData
inputFields.description: [Internal] The body content of the need as a Prosemirror document JSON string. Cannot be used together with body.

inputFields.name: attachmentUrl
inputFields.description: A URL to create an attachment from and associate with this customer need as its source.

inputFields.name: createAsUser
inputFields.description: Create the need attributed to an external user with the provided name. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating needs in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: displayIconUrl
inputFields.description: Avatar URL for the external user specified in `createAsUser`. Can only be used in conjunction with `createAsUser`. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating needs in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the customer need was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.

types.name: CustomerNeedCreateFromAttachmentInput
types.description: Input for creating a customer need from an existing issue attachment. If the attachment already has an archived need, it will be unarchived instead.

inputFields.name: attachmentId
inputFields.description: The UUID of the existing issue attachment to create a customer need from.

types.name: CustomerNeedUpdatePayload
types.description: Return type for customer need update mutations, including any related needs that were also updated.

fields.name: lastSyncId
fields.description: The identifier of the last sync operation.

fields.name: need
fields.description: The customer need entity that was created or updated by the mutation.

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the operation was successful.

fields.name: updatedRelatedNeeds
fields.description: Additional customer needs from the same customer on the same issue/project that were updated when applyPriorityToRelatedNeeds was set.

types.name: CustomerNeedUpdateInput
types.description: Input for updating a customer need. Supports reassigning the customer, moving to a different issue or project, changing priority, and updating body content.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: An optional identifier in UUID v4 format. If provided, will be set as the customer need's ID.

inputFields.name: customerId
inputFields.description: The UUID of the customer to reassign this need to. Cannot be used together with customerExternalId.

inputFields.name: customerExternalId
inputFields.description: The external system ID of the customer to reassign this need to. Cannot be used together with customerId.

inputFields.name: issueId
inputFields.description: The issue to move this need to. Accepts a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123'). The need's attachment will also be moved to the target issue.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: [INTERNAL] The project to move this need to.

inputFields.name: priority
inputFields.description: Whether the customer need is important or not. 0 = Not important, 1 = Important.

inputFields.name: applyPriorityToRelatedNeeds
inputFields.description: When true and priority is also set, applies the same priority update to all other needs from the same customer on the same issue or project.

inputFields.name: body
inputFields.description: The updated body content of the need in Markdown format. Set to null to clear the body. Cannot be used together with bodyData.

inputFields.name: bodyData
inputFields.description: [Internal] The updated body content of the need as a Prosemirror document JSON string. Set to null to clear the body. Cannot be used together with body.

inputFields.name: attachmentUrl
inputFields.description: A URL to create a new attachment from and set as the source for this customer need. Replaces any existing manually-added attachment.

types.name: CustomerCreateInput

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.description: The display name of the customer organization.

inputFields.name: domains
inputFields.description: The email domains associated with this customer (e.g., 'acme.com'). Public email domains are not allowed. Defaults to an empty array.

inputFields.name: externalIds
inputFields.description: Identifiers for this customer in external systems (e.g., CRM IDs). Defaults to an empty array.

inputFields.name: slackChannelId
inputFields.description: The ID of the Slack channel to link to this customer.

inputFields.description: The identifier of the user to assign as the owner of the customer.

inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer status to set.

inputFields.name: revenue
inputFields.description: The annual revenue generated by the customer, in dollars.

inputFields.name: size
inputFields.description: The size of the customer organization (e.g., number of employees).

inputFields.name: tierId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer tier to assign.

inputFields.name: logoUrl
inputFields.description: The URL of the customer's logo image.

inputFields.name: mainSourceId
inputFields.description: The primary external source ID for customers with multiple sources. Must be one of the values provided in externalIds.

inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer status to set.

inputFields.name: revenue
inputFields.description: The annual revenue generated by the customer, in dollars.

inputFields.name: size
inputFields.description: The size of the customer organization (e.g., number of employees).

inputFields.name: tierId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer tier to assign.

inputFields.name: logoUrl
inputFields.description: The URL of the customer's logo image.

inputFields.name: mainSourceId
inputFields.description: The primary external source ID for customers with multiple sources. Must be one of the values in externalIds.

types.name: CustomerUpsertInput
types.description: Input for upserting a customer. Matches against existing customers using id, externalId, slackChannelId, or domains. Creates a new customer if no match is found.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. Used to match an existing customer for upsert.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the customer. Required when creating a new customer.

inputFields.name: domains
inputFields.description: The email domains associated with this customer.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: An external system identifier for this customer. Used for matching existing customers during upsert.

inputFields.name: slackChannelId
inputFields.description: The ID of the Slack channel to link to this customer.

inputFields.name: ownerId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the user to assign as the owner of the customer.

inputFields.name: statusId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer status to set.

inputFields.name: revenue
inputFields.description: The annual revenue generated by the customer, in dollars.

inputFields.name: size
inputFields.description: The size of the customer organization (e.g., number of employees).

inputFields.name: tierId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the customer tier to assign. Cannot be used together with tierName.

inputFields.name: logoUrl
inputFields.description: The URL of the customer's logo image.

inputFields.name: tierName
inputFields.description: The name of the customer tier to assign. A new tier will be created if one with this name does not exist. Cannot be used together with tierId.

types.description: Input for creating a customer status in the workspace's customer lifecycle flow.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The internal name of the status. At least one of name or displayName must be provided.

inputFields.name: color
inputFields.description: The color of the status indicator in the UI, as a HEX string (e.g., '#ff0000').

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: An optional description explaining what this status represents.

inputFields.name: position
inputFields.description: The sort position of the status in the workspace's customer lifecycle flow. If omitted or colliding, a position is automatically assigned at the end.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: The user-facing display name of the status. At least one of name or displayName must be provided.

inputFields.name: description

inputFields.name: position
inputFields.description: The updated sort position of the status in the workspace's customer lifecycle flow.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: The updated user-facing display name of the status.

types.name: IssueCreateInput
types.description: Input for creating a new issue. At minimum, a team must be specified. A title is required unless a template is provided. All other fields are optional and will use defaults from the team or template if not specified.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: The title of the issue.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The issue description in markdown format.

inputFields.name: descriptionData
inputFields.description: [Internal] The issue description as a Prosemirror document.

inputFields.name: assigneeId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the user to assign the issue to.

inputFields.name: delegateId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the agent user to delegate the issue to.

inputFields.name: parentId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the parent issue. Can be a UUID or issue identifier (e.g., 'LIN-123').

inputFields.name: priority
inputFields.description: The priority of the issue. 0 = No priority, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Medium, 4 = Low.

inputFields.name: estimate
inputFields.description: The estimated complexity of the issue.

inputFields.name: subscriberIds
inputFields.description: The identifiers of the users subscribing to this ticket.

inputFields.name: labelIds
inputFields.description: The identifiers of the issue labels associated with this ticket.

inputFields.name: teamId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the team associated with the issue.

inputFields.name: cycleId
inputFields.description: The cycle associated with the issue.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: The project associated with the issue.

inputFields.name: projectMilestoneId
inputFields.description: The project milestone associated with the issue.

inputFields.name: lastAppliedTemplateId
inputFields.description: The ID of the last template applied to the issue.

inputFields.name: stateId
inputFields.description: The team state of the issue.

inputFields.name: referenceCommentId
inputFields.description: The comment the issue is referencing.

inputFields.name: sourceCommentId
inputFields.description: The comment the issue is created from.

inputFields.name: sourcePullRequestCommentId
inputFields.description: [Internal] The pull request comment the issue is created from.

inputFields.name: sortOrder
inputFields.description: The position of the issue related to other issues.

inputFields.name: prioritySortOrder
inputFields.description: The position of the issue related to other issues, when ordered by priority.

inputFields.name: subIssueSortOrder
inputFields.description: The position of the issue in parent's sub-issue list.

inputFields.name: dueDate
inputFields.description: The date at which the issue is due.

inputFields.name: createAsUser
inputFields.description: Create issue as a user with the provided name. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating issues in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: displayIconUrl
inputFields.description: Provide an external user avatar URL. Can only be used in conjunction with the `createAsUser` options. This option is only available to OAuth applications creating comments in `actor=app` mode.

inputFields.name: preserveSortOrderOnCreate
inputFields.description: Whether the passed sort order should be preserved.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the issue was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.

inputFields.name: slaBreachesAt
inputFields.description: [Internal] The time at which an issue will be considered in breach of SLA.

inputFields.name: slaStartedAt
inputFields.description: [Internal] The time at which the issue's SLA was started.

inputFields.name: templateId
inputFields.description: The identifier of a template the issue should be created from. If other values are provided in the input, they will override template values.

inputFields.name: completedAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the issue was completed (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past and after createdAt. Cannot be provided with an incompatible workflow state.

inputFields.name: slaType
inputFields.description: The SLA day count type for the issue. Whether SLA should be business days only or calendar days (default).

inputFields.name: useDefaultTemplate
inputFields.description: Whether to use the default template for the team. When set to true, the default template of this team based on user's membership will be applied.

inputFields.name: releaseIds
inputFields.description: The identifiers of the releases to associate with this issue.

inputFields.name: inheritsSharedAccess
inputFields.description: [Internal] Whether this issue should inherit shared access from its parent issue. Set to false to opt out of automatic shared access inheritance when creating a sub-issue.

fields.description: The workspace domain that was created or updated.

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the operation was successful.

types.name: OrganizationDomain
types.description: A verified email domain associated with a workspace. Domains are used for automatic team joining, SSO/SAML authentication, and controlling workspace access. Each domain has an authentication type (general or SAML) and can be verified via email or DNS.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: identityProvider
fields.description: The identity provider the domain belongs to.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The domain name (e.g., 'example.com').

fields.name: verified
fields.description: Whether the domain has been verified via email verification.

fields.name: verificationEmail
fields.description: The email address used to verify this domain. Null if the domain was verified via DNS or has not been verified.

fields.name: creator
fields.description: The user who added the domain.

fields.name: authType
fields.description: The authentication type this domain is used for. 'general' means standard email-based auth, 'saml' means SAML SSO authentication.

fields.name: claimed
fields.description: Whether the domain was claimed by the workspace through DNS TXT record verification. Claimed domains provide stronger ownership proof than email verification and enable additional features like preventing users from creating new workspaces.

fields.name: disableOrganizationCreation
fields.description: Whether users with email addresses from this domain are prevented from creating new workspaces. Can only be set on claimed domains.

types.name: OrganizationDomainAuthType
types.description: What type of auth is the domain used for.

inputFields.name: organizationDomainId
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format of the domain being verified.

inputFields.name: verificationCode
inputFields.description: The verification code sent via email.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: identityProviderId
inputFields.description: The identity provider to which to add the domain.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The domain name to add.

inputFields.name: verificationEmail
inputFields.description: The email address to which to send the verification code.

inputFields.name: authType
inputFields.description: The authentication type this domain is for.

inputFields.name: disableOrganizationCreation
inputFields.description: Prevent users with this domain to create new workspaces. Only allowed to set on claimed domains!

types.name: ProjectMilestoneMovePayload

fields.description: The identifier of the last sync operation.

fields.description: The project milestone that was created or updated.

fields.description: Whether the operation was successful.

fields.name: previousIssueTeamIds
fields.description: A snapshot of the issues that were moved to new teams, if the user selected to do it, containing an array of mappings between an issue and its previous team. Store on the client to use for undoing a previous milestone move.

fields.name: previousProjectTeamIds
fields.description: A snapshot of the project that had new teams added to it, if the user selected to do it, containing an array of mappings between a project and its previous teams. Store on the client to use for undoing a previous milestone move.

fields.name: issueId
fields.description: The issue id in this relationship, you can use * as wildcard if all issues are being moved to the same team

fields.name: teamId
fields.description: The team id in this relationship

fields.description: The project id

fields.name: teamIds
fields.description: The team ids for the project

types.name: ProjectMilestoneMoveInput
types.description: [Internal] Input for moving a project milestone to another project.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the project to move the milestone to.

inputFields.name: newIssueTeamId
inputFields.description: The team id to move the attached issues to. This is needed when there is a mismatch between a project's teams and the milestone's issues' teams. Either this or addIssueTeamToProject is required in that situation to resolve constraints.

inputFields.name: addIssueTeamToProject
inputFields.description: Whether to add each milestone issue's team to the project. This is needed when there is a mismatch between a project's teams and the milestone's issues' teams. Either this or newIssueTeamId is required in that situation to resolve constraints.

inputFields.name: undoIssueTeamIds
inputFields.description: A list of issue id to team ids, used for undoing a previous milestone move where the specified issues were moved from the specified teams.

inputFields.name: undoProjectTeamIds
inputFields.description: A mapping of project id to a previous set of team ids, used for undoing a previous milestone move where the specified teams were added to the project.

types.description: [Internal] Used for ProjectMilestoneMoveInput to describe a mapping between an issue and its team.

inputFields.description: The issue id in this relationship, you can use * as wildcard if all issues are being moved to the same team

inputFields.name: teamId
inputFields.description: The team id in this relationship

types.name: PushSubscription
types.description: A device registration for receiving push notifications. Each PushSubscription represents a specific browser or mobile device endpoint where a user has opted in to receive real-time push notifications. A user may have multiple push subscriptions across different devices and browsers.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

types.name: PushSubscriptionCreateInput
types.description: Input for creating a push subscription to receive push notifications on a device or browser.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: data
inputFields.description: The push subscription data in stringified JSON format. For web subscriptions, this must contain keys, endpoint, and expirationTime fields per the Web Push API specification. For mobile subscriptions, this contains the device token.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: The type of push subscription: 'web' for browser-based Web Push API, 'apple' for Apple Push Notification service (or 'appleDevelopment' for sandbox), or 'firebase' for Firebase Cloud Messaging (Android).

types.name: PushSubscriptionType
types.description: The different push subscription types.

inputFields.description: Identifier of the release pipeline.

inputFields.name: releaseIds
inputFields.description: Explicit release IDs to include. Mutually exclusive with rangeFromReleaseId/rangeToReleaseId — exactly one of the two shapes must be provided.

inputFields.name: rangeFromReleaseId
inputFields.description: Oldest release (by createdAt) to include. When paired with rangeToReleaseId, expands to every release in the pipeline within the createdAt window.

inputFields.description: Newest release (by createdAt) to include. Paired with rangeFromReleaseId.

inputFields.description: Optional user-supplied title.

inputFields.description: The release note body as markdown.

types.name: PullRequestReferenceInput
types.description: A reference to a pull request by its repository owner, name, and pull request number. Used during release sync to look up pull requests and associate their linked issues with the release.

inputFields.name: repositoryName

types.name: RepositoryDataInput
types.description: Metadata about the source code repository from which a release is being synced, including the hosting provider and repository coordinates.

inputFields.name: owner
inputFields.description: The owner of the repository (e.g., organization or user name).

inputFields.name: name

inputFields.name: provider
inputFields.description: The VCS provider hosting the repository (e.g., 'github', 'gitlab', 'bitbucket').

inputFields.name: url
inputFields.description: The base URL of the repository on the hosting provider (e.g., 'https://github.com/linear/linear-app').

types.name: ReleaseDebugSinkInput
types.description: Diagnostic data captured during release sync, including inspected commits, discovered issue references, and pull request metadata. Stored on the release for debugging release association issues.

inputFields.description: List of commit SHAs that were inspected.

inputFields.name: issues

inputFields.name: revertedIssues

inputFields.name: pullRequests
inputFields.description: Pull request debug information.

inputFields.name: includePaths
inputFields.description: List of paths applied during commit scanning.

types.name: ReleaseCreateInput
types.description: The input for creating a release.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The identifier in UUID v4 format. If none is provided, the backend will generate one.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the release.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the release.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The version of the release.

inputFields.name: commitSha
inputFields.description: The commit SHA associated with this release.

inputFields.name: pipelineId
inputFields.description: The identifier of the pipeline this release belongs to.

inputFields.name: stageId
inputFields.description: The current stage of the release. Defaults to the first 'completed' stage for continuous pipelines, or the first 'started' stage for scheduled pipelines.

inputFields.name: startDate
inputFields.description: The estimated start date of the release.

inputFields.name: targetDate
inputFields.description: The estimated completion date of the release.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the release was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.

inputFields.name: startedAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the release was started (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past and after createdAt.

inputFields.name: completedAt
inputFields.description: The time at which the release was completed (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past and after createdAt. Cannot be provided with an incompatible stage.

types.name: DocumentContentDraft
types.description: A draft revision of document content, pending user review. Each user can have at most one draft per document content. Drafts are seeded from the live document state and stored as base64-encoded Yjs state updates, allowing independent editing without affecting the published document until the user explicitly applies their changes.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was created.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description:
The last time at which the entity was meaningfully updated. This is the same as the creation time if the entity hasn't
    been updated after creation.

fields.name: archivedAt
fields.description: The time at which the entity was archived. Null if the entity has not been archived.

fields.name: documentContent
fields.description: The document content that this draft is a revision of.

fields.name: documentContentId
fields.description: The identifier of the document content that this draft is a revision of.

fields.name: contentState
fields.description: The draft content state as a base64-encoded Yjs state update. This represents the user's in-progress edits that have not yet been applied to the live document.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who created or owns this draft.

fields.name: userId
fields.description: The identifier of the user who owns this draft.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
